#  Summer VolunTEEN Program 
![VolunTEEN banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=67170)
# Applications open February 2nd!
[CLICK HERE](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Community-Services-4/2026-VolunTEEN-Program-Application-395) to Apply
The Anaheim Community Services Department provides teens, 13 - 17 years of age, the opportunity to give back to their community and earn service hours through the Summer VolunTEEN Program. Teens can volunteer at various summer programs throughout Anaheim such as:
  * [Summer Day Camp and Wilderness Survival Camp at Oak Canyon Nature Center](https://www.anaheim.net/1110/OAKS-Day-Camp)
  * [Tiny Tots Academy](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  * [CampVenture](https://www.anaheim.net/2285/CampVenture)
  * [Summer Nights Under the Stars at Pearson Park Amphitheatre](https://www.anaheim.net/1432/Pearson-Park-Amphitheatre)
  * [Haskett Library](https://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/Facilities/Facility/Details/Haskett-Branch-Library-20)
  * [Ponderosa Joint-Use Library](https://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/Facilities/Facility/Details/Ponderosa-JointUse-Library-19)
  * [Euclid Library](https://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/Facilities/Facility/Details/Euclid-Branch-Library-21)
  * [Canyon Hills Library](https://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/Facilities/Facility/Details/Canyon-Hills-Branch-Library-24)
  * [East Anaheim Gymnasium](https://www.anaheim.net/966/East-Anaheim-Gymnasium)


### Eligibility
Applicants must be 13 years of age. Because of limited space, Anaheim residents and students of Anaheim schools will be given priority placement. Applications will be available beginning Monday, February 2nd. **Please note: Due to high volume of applications, no application will be accepted after the deadline, April 10th at 5pm.**
###   

  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/4829/Summer-VolunTEEN-Program#tabddc59a5e-69b6-4bc9-98ae-028bdcd922bd_0)


  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/4829/Summer-VolunTEEN-Program#tabddc59a5e-69b6-4bc9-98ae-028bdcd922bd_0)


  1. Begin application using link above.
  2. Submit TB clearance slip dated within the last two years (2024 - present will be accepted)
  3. Select a fingerprinting appointment. 
  4. Select an orientation date. 
  5. Fill in contact information. 
  6. Select preferred program and location and desired weeks to volunteer.
  7. Complete waivers. 
  8. Submit application.


### Current TB Clearance & Fingerprinting Background Checks
The Community Services Department is required to have volunteers' TB clearance on file. All applicants are required to submit a copy of a TB clearance slip dated within the last two years (2024 - present will be accepted). Applicants are responsible for the cost of the test.
State Law AB 1663 requires all recreation program volunteers be fingerprinted for a background check and cleared by the California Department of Justice before placement. Fingerprinting is available at no-cost and must be completed by City of Anaheim staff.
### Fingerprinting Appointments & Locations
Fingerprints are scheduled by appointment only. Appointments may be made by contacting the Volunteer Coordinator at (714) 765-4994 or VolunteerOffice@anaheim.net. All appointments will take place at City Hall.
### REMINDERS
  * Fingerprinting is for **NEW** VolunTEEN Program applicants only. Returning VolunTEENS do not need to be fingerprinted.
  * Applicants **MUST** bring a current, government-issued I.D. photo card (state I.D., driver's license, US passport) to their fingerprinting appointment.


### Application Submission & Deadline
Completed applications (including TB tests) will be accepted beginning Monday, February 2nd, 2026. Applications will only be accepted online.
Site selection priority will be given to those who have completed the registration process including attendance at a VolunTEEN Orientation. Applications will be accepted from February 2nd until April 10th, or until program fills to capacity.
The last day to submit a complete application is Friday, April 10th - applications must be received by 5:00 p.m. to be considered. The program is limited; partial or incomplete applications will not be accepted.
### Assignment & Hour Confirmation Letters
Volunteer Assignment emails will be sent to VolunTEENS by May 30th. At the end of the 2026 Program, all VolunTEENS will receive a letter confirming the number of community service hours performed. Hour confirmation emails will be sent by August 30th.
### Questions & Additional Information
For questions or additional information, please contact the Volunteer Coordinator at (714) 765-4994 or volunteeroffice@anaheim.net.

  6. Scam Alert


#  Scam Alert 
### Anaheim Public Utilities never makes unsolicited customer calls or visits asking for immediate payment.
The most common scam tactics are to pose as utility employees by phone call, email, text message, or personal visit, and threatening to shut off water and/or power unless payment is made immediately.
If you receive this type of threat, hang up or close the door, and then call the number on your utility bill or call the Anaheim Police Department at 714-765-1900 to report suspicious activity. 
## **I AM A VICTIM OF UTILITY FRAUD – WHAT DO I DO?**
###   

  1. If someone is knocking on your door and threatening you, close the door and call 911.
  2. If you have made a payment to a scammer, report the crime to the Anaheim Police Department at 714-765-1900 or file a report in person at the Main or East Police Station front counter(s) for Financial Fraud.
  3. Call Anaheim Public Utilities at the number on your utility bill, and we will work with you on your account to avoid disconnection.


![Scam Alert](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=21197)
##  **DON’T FALL FOR THESE SCAM TACTICS!**  

  * Scammers instruct residents and businesses to purchase a prepaid card to solicit payment in order to avoid disconnection of their water and/or electric service. 
  * Utility impersonators may use what’s called “spoofing software,” which allows them to falsely display the Anaheim Public Utilities phone number on your caller ID. 
  * Customers are instructed to call the impersonator back at a telephone number different from the customer service number listed on the back of their utility bill to give them the prepaid card number. 


  

## **ANAHEIM PUBLIC UTILITIES PAYMENT PRACTICES**
##   

**Anaheim representatives will never** make unsolicited calls to customers asking for payment information over the phone, nor will our staff visit your home to solicit payment. Customers can pay their utility bills in one of the following ways:
  * **By mail** to Anaheim Public Utilities, P.O. Box 3222, Anaheim, CA 92803-3222 
  * **In-person** through the Anaheim West Tower drop box located outside of 201 S. Anaheim Blvd.
  * **Through our automated phone system** , by calling the phone number on your utility bill 
  * **Through our secure**[**online portal**](https://myapu.anaheim.net/iwr/user/login.seam) that is password-protected


  

_If customers are past-due on their bills, you may request a payment extension or assistance from a number of non-profit agencies._  
_The best way to ensure you will not be disconnected is to call a customer service representative and discuss ways to avoid service disruptions._
_For past-due bills, Anaheim Public Utilities will mail at least one past-due notice and one disconnection notice before disrupting your service._  
_As a not-for-profit agency, this practice is to ensure that the burden of unpaid bills does not fall on other customers._  
  

## **TIPS TO AVOID FRAUDULENT ACTIVITY AND KEEP YOUR FAMILY SAFE**
  

  * Be cautious about the personal information you share online or over the phone – do not give your financial information if someone is calling you for payment.
  * Make sure to shred all bills you are throwing in the trash.
  * Anaheim Public Utilities accepts cash, check, credit card (Visa, MasterCard, or Discover), money order, or payment via checking account.   
Prepaid cards are not accepted.
  * If you receive a disconnection notification by mail, call Anaheim Public Utilities to discuss ways to avoid a service disruption.  
  



## **STEPS ANAHEIM IS TAKING TO ADDRESS SCAMS**
  

  1. Toll-free numbers that have been reported by customers as fraudulent have been reported and shut down.
  2. When you call Anaheim Public Utilities to make a payment, our representatives will not take your financial information; instead, they will transfer you to an automated payment system. 
  3. Anaheim Public Utilities is working closely with the Anaheim Police Department on utility fraud and conducts investigations accordingly.
  4. Anaheim Public Utilities has a presence at more than 70 events per year and is always willing to speak with community groups to share fraud awareness information. Contact us at the phone number on your utility bill to request a representative to meet with your complex or neighborhood.

  6. Street Sweeping


![City of Anaheim Street Sweeping page with street sweeping truck on street](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=48565)
Anaheim's street sweeping service cleans our streets and helps keep debris and pollution from flowing into storm drains and waterways and getting to our ocean and coastline. That's something we should all embrace and make way for.
That's a big reason why we sweep our streets. But it's not the only one.
  * Street sweeping cleans and enhances neighborhoods and brings better quality of life
  * Federal and state clean water regulations require Anaheim and other cities to sweep streets
  * You pay for it! All residents pay for street sweeping through their utility bills[](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/446)[  
](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/446)


##  Street Sweeping Schedule  
  

Street sweeping takes place in neighborhoods Mondays through Thursdays.
Fridays see street sweeping in alleyways and major streets with ticketing only if signs are posted.
Morning routes Monday through Thursday are from 8 a.m. to noon. Afternoon routes are from noon to 4 p.m.
The street sweeper can come anytime during the hours of morning or afternoon routes.
Should the sweeper come after noon on a morning route or after 4 p.m. on an afternoon route, there is no ticketing.
[View a map](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/446) of Anaheim street sweeping days.
For street sweeping questions, please call (714) 765-6860![phone icon](https://www.anaheim.net/480/Street-Sweeping). 
To request an exemption for a disabled vehicle, please call (714) 765-4444![phone icon](https://www.anaheim.net/480/Street-Sweeping)
### SWEEPING IN WEATHER
![Expect street sweeping in weather with sunny clear skies](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=48561)
![street sweeping headexpect street sweeping to take place in sunny and windy conditionsers \(3\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=48563)
![Expect street sweeping in light rain with no water buildup ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=48562)
![expect street sweeping to be canceled in heavy rain with water buildup on streets](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=48564)
## 2025 Street Sweeping Holiday Schedule
|  |   
---|---|---  
January  | New Years Day, Thursday, Jan. 1, 2026 | No street sweeping, no citations  
January | Martin Luther King Jr. Day, Monday, Jan. 19, 2026 | No street sweeping, no citations  
February | Presidents Day, Monday, Feb. 16, 2026 | No street sweeping, no citations  
March | Cesar Chavez Day, Tuesday, March 31, 2026 | Sweeping, no citations  
May | Memorial Day, Monday, May 26, 2026 | No street sweeping, no citations  
June | Juneteenth, Friday, June 19, 2026 | Sweeping, no citations  
July  | Fourth of July, Friday, July 3 — observed as July 4 falls on Saturday | No street sweeping, no citations  
September | Labor Day, Monday, Sept. 7, 2026 | No street sweeping, no citations  
October | Indigenous Peoples Day, Monday, Oct. 12, 2026 | Sweeping, no citations  
November | Veterans Day, Wednesday, Nov. 11, 2026 | No street sweeping, no citations  
November | Thanksgiving, Thursday, Nov. 26, 2026 | No street sweeping, no citations  
November | Day after Thanksgiving, Friday, Nov. 27, 2026 | No street sweeping, no citations  
December | Christmas, Friday, Dec. 25, 2026 | No street sweeping, no citations  
January 2026 | New Years Day, Friday, Jan. 1, 2027 | No street sweeping, no citations  
## Citations
## Citing vehicles for street sweeping is the last thing we want to do. If everyone moved vehicles to make way for street sweeping, that would be great. But we know that doesn't happen without citations.
##   

##  We cite for street sweeping because of the importance of the service to clean neighborhoods, protecting our environment and because all residents pay for the service through their utility bills — and we want to ensure you are getting what you pay for.  
![Ocean wash](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=50908)  

When we see heavy rain, trash and debris makes its way into our stormwater system, into waterways and ultimately to the ocean, with tragic pollution impacts for beaches and marine life. The photo here shows trash washed to Seal Beach after Tropical Storm Hilary in August 2023.
That is something we all should be concerned about and work to prevent. Street sweeping is one of the best ways to do that.
## We thank you for making way for our street sweepers and helping to keep your neighborhoods and larger environment clean.
##   

Citations are issued where signs are posted indicating "No Parking for Street Sweeping."
Signs are posted every 250 feet on main streets and are also at the entrances to neighborhoods.
Citations are issued under California Vehicle Code section 22507.6.  

##  Street sweeping citations must be addressed within 21 calendar days of the issue date.  
  

## For citation inquiries, please call (888) 255-9711![phone icon](https://www.anaheim.net/480/Street-Sweeping)
##    
To request a street sweeping exemption for special circumstances, please call (714) 765-4444![phone icon](https://www.anaheim.net/480/Street-Sweeping)

  6. North Basin Project 


![logo updated](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=19780)
The North Basin project is a proposed clean-up of groundwater led by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency that touches on parts of north Anaheim. 
The project is in the early phases and is set to play out over a number of years.
It is designed to address industrial contamination dating back to the 1950s and to ensure continued safe drinking water in north Orange County.
There are no concerns about the safety and quality of Anaheim’s drinking water. 
You can learn more about the North Basin project below or by clicking on our fact sheet below.
Questions? Call Anaheim Public Utilities at (714) 765-4311.
![Learn more banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44089)

#  External Wireless Networks 
Major cell phone companies (AT&T, Verizon, and T-Mobile) are working to improve service for their customers in Anaheim. As part of these improvements, they are replacing some of the streetlight poles to allow installing their wireless equipment inside an enclosure at the top. These installations are referred to as Small Cell Sites. To streamline the process, APU has adopted a standard license agreement and wireless-ready streetlight poles shown under the following links:
  

  * [Sample Street Light License Agreement](http://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/23292/Sample-Street-Light-License-Agreement-and-Rate-for-Wireless-Deployment)
  * [L 146: Marbelite Wireless-Ready Pole](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/30056/L-146-Marbelite-Wireless-Ready-Pole)
  * [L 144: Tranditionaire Wireless-Ready Pole](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/30057/L-144-Traditionaire-Wireless-Ready-Pole)
  * [L 147: Proposed Anaheim Resort Wireless-Ready Pole](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/30058/L-147-Proposed-Anaheim-Resort-Wireless-Ready-Pole)
  * [L 148: Downtown Anaheim Wireless-Ready Pole](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/30134/L-148--Downtown-Anaheim-Wireless-Ready-Pole)


Here's what a typical installation on two types of Anaheim's street light poles look like: 
![Street Light Pole](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=30191)
![Cell on Pole ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=30052)
Approximately a week before commencing construction, these cell phone companies distribute their flyer to those residents in the immediate vicinity of the proposed installation. Listed below are sample flyers from AT&T, Verizon, and Crown Castle with their contact info:
  * [AT&T Notification Flyer ](http://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/30055/ATT-Notification-Flyer)
  * [Verizon Notification Flyer](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/30053/Verizon-Notification-Flyer)
  * [Crown Castle Notification Flyer](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/30054/Mobilitie-Notification-Flyer)


### The proposed wireless sites on streetlight poles are displayed as dots on the map.
[Report Cell Phone and High Speed Internet Service Concerns](https://gis.anaheim.net/portal/apps/experiencebuilder/experience/?id=acee779cb13c45fda26570220c5e4afc)
* * *
For questions, please contact Fred Barvarz at 714-765-4191 or fbarvarz@anaheim.net.
* * *
For more information on wireless improvements by various cell companies providers, please refer to the below links:  
  

  * [Verizon](https://www.verizon.com/about/our-company/5g/what-5g)
  * [AT&T](https://www.att.com/5g/consumer/)
  * [T-Mobile](https://www.t-mobile.com/5g)
  * [Sprint](https://www.sprint.com/en/landings/5g.html)
  * [Crown Castle](https://www.crowncastle.com/communities/5g-information)
  * [Mobilitie](http://www.mobilitie.com/connectedcity/index.html)

#  State of the City 
![Web main](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=25202)
## An annual Anaheim tradition
Each year, Anaheim's mayor gathers with the community to share new ideas and celebrate recent accomplishments in the State of the City luncheon.
The **2019 State of the City** , the first featuring **Mayor Harry Sidhu** , took place **March 5** at the **City National Grove of Anaheim**. 
You can read Mayor Sidhu's speech at the link to the right. 
Stay tuned for a link to the complete State of the City video in the coming days.
You can visit an archive of State of the City speeches and videos to the upper left.

#  Hermosa Village Teen Center 
The Project S.A.Y. (Support Anaheim's Youth) Hermosa Village Teen Center is open to Junior High and High School youth, ages 13-17. The Teen Center is open Monday, Wednesday, Friday from 2:30 p.m. - 5:30 p.m. Teen activities include: Leadership activities, drop-in sports, homework help, computer lab, fitness programs, development workshops, tutoring, and youth excursions,
[![community services interior logo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42964)](https://www.anaheim.net/6094)

  4. [Ponderosa Teen Room](https://www.anaheim.net/2334/Ponderosa-Teen-Room)

#  Planning & Building 
[![planning banner Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=58107)](https://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/DocumentCenter/View/57943/Updated-notice-12-26-24?bidId=)
[![Planning page graphic Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=40201)](https://aca-prod.accela.com/anaheim/default.aspx)
[![Video button Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=40335)](https://youtu.be/-GKS6K_7t6Q)
Counter Hours
  

**Building Services**
Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday  
8:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.  
  
Thursday  
Delayed opening 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.
  

**Business License Services**
Monday - Friday  
8:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.
  

**Planning Services**
Monday - Friday  
8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.
  

Phone Hours
Monday - Friday
8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.
### [_Building Division_](https://anaheim.net/5252/Building-Division)
  * For available Building Division services call (714) 765-5153 or visit [Anaheim.net/Building](http://www.anaheim.net/building).


### [_Business License Division_](https://anaheim.net/494/Business-License)
  * For available Business License services call (714) 765-5194 or visit [Anaheim.net/BusinessLicense](http://www.anaheim.net/BusinessLicense).


### [_Code Enforcement_](https://anaheim.net/682/Code-Enforcement)
  * Questions or service requests, call: (714) 765-5158 or email CPreservation@anaheim.net
  * Request virtual inspections via our website [here](https://www.anaheim.net/5443/Virtual-Inspections).


### [_Planning and Zoning_](https://anaheim.net/691/Planning-Zoning)
  * [Discretionary Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/5755/) and [Administrative Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/5756/) _GoPost_ online portal. By using _GoPost_ you can submit applications online to the Planning Division and upload all plans and supporting documents. Application materials may also be submitted in-person by appointment at the Planning Services Division Counter at City Hall. Digital format of application materials are required for in-person submittal. Contact Eibet Olmedo at EOlmedo@anaheim.net or 714-765-5139. All planning application and filing deadline information can be found on the [Forms & Applications website](https://www.anaheim.net/879/).


### _Public Record Requests_
  * Submit an online public record for planning services [here](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1869/Records-Request?bidId=).
  * Email your planning records request to PRecords@anaheim.net.
  * For other department records [click here](http://anaheim.net/2153/Request-a-Public-Record).

#  Solar for Schools 
The Solar for Schools Program installs solar on local public schools throughout Anaheim. Nine schools participated in the program as shown below. Construction was completed in December 2018.   
  
Solar energy produced from APU-owned systems is available to income-qualified residential customers for financial assistance on their bills, [learn more](http://www.anaheim.net/5083/Income-Qualified-Solar-Power-Discount-Pr).  

**SCHOOL​​​**  
| **SCHOOL DISTRICT** | **TYPE OF SOLAR** | **SOLAR PV SIZE (kW) DC**  
---|---|---|---  
Franklin Elementary | Anaheim Elementary | Carport | 108  
Betsy Ross Elementary | Anaheim Elementary | Carport | 115  
Danbrook Elementary | Centralia Unified | Playground Shelter | 94  
Centralia Elementary | Centralia Unified | Lunch Shelter | 67  
Dr. Jonas Salk Elementary | Magnolia School District | Carport | 94  
Lord Baden-Powell Elementary | Magnolia School District | Carport | 101  
Esperanza High School | Placentia Yorba Linda Unified | Carport | 742  
Hansen Elementary | Savanna Unified | Carport | 101  
Cerritos Elementary | Savanna Unified | Shade Structure | 122  
**TOTAL** | **1,543**

  2. ![Solar](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=23083)
  3. ![Solar](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=23085)


**Project Contact:**
Mark Brownrigg, 714-765-4182

#  Animal Care and Control 
![animal page header](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=22800)
OC Animal Care provides animal control services for the City of Anaheim. 
For general questions, contact:  
OC Animal Care   
(714) 935-6848  
[www.ocpetinfo.com  
](http://www.ocpetinfo.com)1630 Victory Rd., Tustin, CA 92782
**For questions or concerns regarding the OC Animal Care contract, please contact the Code Enforcement Office at (714) 765-5158.**
Outlined below are the specific components included within each of the services:
**Shelter Services:**
**Adoptions**
There are many cute dogs, cats, rabbits and other animals looking for their forever home available for adoption at OC Animal Care's shelter in Tustin.
View all adoptable pets at petadoption.ocpetinfo.com. Adoption fees vary, please [CLICK HERE](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/sites/ocac/files/2021-04/27561.pdf) for full details.
To schedule an adoption visit, call (714) 935-6848, and mention the Animal ID# for any specific animal you might be interested in. Walk-ins welcome!
**Lost, Stray and Injured Animals**
OC Animal Care takes in sick, injured, ill, aggressive animals, along with stray dogs, 7 days/week. 
To report a lost, stray or injured animal, please call (714) 935-6848.
If you have recently lost a pet, please visit the following websites to see photos and information regarding lost/stray animals currently at the shelter as well as reported found pets that are not currently at the shelter:
      * [Lost pets currently at OC Animal Care shelter:](https://petadoption.ocpetinfo.com/LostFound)
      * [Reported found pets not at OC Animal Care:](https://petadoption.ocpetinfo.com/FoundOCAC)


Losing your beloved four-legged friend can be very upsetting. [CLICK HERE](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/lost-pets/tips-finding-lost-pet) to view OC Animal Care tips on how to find your pet.
**Caring for Animals at the Shelter**
As a municipal shelter, OC Animal Care is a way station for animals, a safe place for them to land while the shelter finds responsible placement for them. The shelter has dedicated staff that have a variety of duties, all of which focus on the animals in their care. All animals in the shelter receive enrichment and attention customized to their individual needs. Every effort is made to enhance the animal’s stay at the shelter and place it quickly into an adoptive home. 
**Veterinary services & Low-Cost Spay/Neuter**
OC Animal Care has on-site veterinary services to provide medical care for animals admitted to the shelter. 
For public services, OC Animal Care partners with Healthcare & Emergency Animal Rescue Team (H.E.A.R.T) provides low-cost mobile pet clinics throughout the year, offering low-cost spay/neuter, vaccinations, microchipping and other services conveniently located in the parking lot of OC Animal Care. For more information, a calendar of clinics, or make an appointment, visit [www.heart4pets.org](https://www.heart4pets.org)
**Volunteer and Foster Programs**
From assisting with animal care and enrichment activities to participating in events and community outreach, OC Animal Care welcomes dedicated individuals who want to lend their time and talents to serve the shelter animals. Learn more about OC Animal Care volunteer opportunities [HERE](http://www.ocpetinfo.com/get-involved)
OC Animal Care’s Foster Caretaker Program has successfully saved the lives of thousands of animals! The foster program buys time and gives a second chance to young or injured puppies, kittens, dogs, and cats or simply gives a shelter pet a break from shelter life. Learn more about OC Animal Care foster opportunities [HERE](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/get-involved/foster)
**Rescue Agencies**
OC Animal Care partners with many sister-shelters and 501(c)3 rescue organizations. Please [CLICK HERE](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/get-involved/adoption-partner-program) to register your group. 
**Euthanasia and disposal of animals**
Public safety is a top priority at OC Animal Care. As such, the shelter does not turn away any behaviorally challenged animal even if another shelter has. Our intent is to find responsible placement for the animals in our care if possible. To do this, we have a consistent behavior evaluation process in place to help find the most successful placement possible for the animals in our care. If your personal pet is in need of euthanasia services, it is recommended that you reach out to your personal veterinarian.
If you are in need of pet disposal services, please call (714) 935-6848.
**Animal Control Services:**
**Emergency response**
OC Animal Care works in conjunction with Anaheim PD and Fire to respond to emergencies involving animals.
**Impounding stray animals**
If you see a stray or injured animal and you cannot locate its owner, call (714) 935-6848 and an Animal Control Officer will respond.
**Animal cruelty, animal bite & vicious dog investigations**
If you observe or suspect someone of abusing or neglecting an animal, immediately contact OC Animal Care at (714) 935-6848 and an Animal Control Officer will respond.
You are required by law to report any bite or scratch from a domestic or wild animal. Report an animal bite or scratch from a domestic or wild animal by calling (714) 796-6421 or [filling out the form](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/sites/ocac/files/import/data/files/73685.pdf).
**Field releases to owner and Take Me Home Program**
Pets currently licensed with OC Animal Care qualify for OC Animal Care's Take Me Home Program. This pet redemption program is offered to pet owners who have had their licensed pets impounded for the first time and requires pet owners to redeem their pet(s) within the first 24 hours. 
In addition, in certain instances a Field officer may give your lost pet a ride home through the Return to Owner Field Program. Call (714) 935-6848 for more information.
**Transportation of injured or deceased animals**
If you discover an injured or deceased animal in your yard or on the road, please call (714) 935-6848.
**Coyotes**
Coyotes are lured to urban areas due to the availability of accessible food sources, such as garbage cans and outdoor pet food, which in turn attract smaller animals that serve as prey for coyotes. But there are steps you can take to keep your home, family and pets safe. Read more on our [coyote page](https://www.anaheim.net/coyotes).
**Bees**
In the event of a bee swarm on your property, it is strongly advised to exercise caution and refrain from disturbing the bees. Promptly contact a local bee removal company for assistance. Should the swarm be located on city property within Anaheim, please dial (714) 765-6860 or utilize the designated 311 hotline accessible from any Anaheim telephone.
**Animal Care Special Services:**
**Animal licensing and renewal, fee collection and payment services.**
All dogs in Orange County over the age of four months are required by law to be licensed and vaccinated against rabies. Residents owning or having custody of any dog must license the dog within 15 days of acquisition or within 15 days after the license becomes due.
Cat licensing is optional and available through OC Animal Care.
Learn more about licensing your pet [HERE](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/license/how-obtain-pet-license)
**Animal canvassing efforts to locate and license unlicensed animals**
OC Animal Care provides digital canvassing efforts for the City of Anaheim. All dog owners in Anaheim are required by law to license their pet. Please [CLICK HERE](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/license/how-obtain-pet-license) for more information.
**Inspection of animal-related businesses**
Routine inspections are conducted to ensure animals are properly cared for and adequate records are kept. If you observe any of the following conditions at an animal-related business, report it to OC Animal Care at (714) 935-6848 for investigation:
      * Cruelty to animals
      * Sick or injured animals
      * Lack of healthful food and potable water 
      * Unsanitary facilities


To view a full list of animal-related business that requires licensing, please [CLICK HERE](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/field-operations/business-licensing)
**Barking Dog Complaints:**
**Submit a barking dog complaint**
In the event of a persistent barking dog in your neighborhood, we recommend initiating a conversation with the respective neighbor to address the matter. Should the issue not be resolved, please report online by clicking [here](https://petadoption.ocpetinfo.com/OnlineFieldReport/?f=1).
For any questions on the form, call (714) 796-6442.
View the full criteria for a Barking Dog or Animal Nuisance violation [HERE](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/page/barking-dog-animal-nuisance-complaint-process)
**Barking Dog & Animal Nuisance Complaint Process**
OC Animal Care facilitates the Barking Dog or Animal Nuisance complaint process, acting as an intermediary between the complainant and responsible person; and issuing a citation if necessary.
OC Animal Care can provide assistance mediating the circumstances of the issue or other information between both parties and can provide suggestions on how to resolve the issue with the animal in question. Citations are issued after the receipt of a second complaint and are based on the sworn affidavit signed by a complainant.
View the full criteria for a Barking Dog or Animal Nuisance violation [HERE](https://www.ocpetinfo.com/page/barking-dog-animal-nuisance-complaint-process)

#  Renewable Energy Resources 
## A Brief History
  
In 1894, the City of Anaheim established the first municipal electric utility in Southern California. Within a year, Anaheim Public Utilities extended energy services to the downtown area and continued generating power until 1916. From 1916 to 1976, Anaheim purchased its power supply from Southern California Edison; however, faced with rising rates from Edison, the city explored electric generation and transmission projects. From 1976 to 2003, it received power from its ownership shares in [conventional resources](https://www.anaheim.net/5089/), including coal, natural gas, and nuclear energy. 
Beginning in 2003, Anaheim shifted towards providing customers with renewable resources. In response to Assembly Bill 32 (2006), which established statewide Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions reduction targets, Anaheim has been increasing its provision of renewable energy to customers while reducing non-renewable energy consumption to help achieve statewide GHG emissions reduction goals. 
Today, we provide electricity to our customers from multiple renewable energy projects across the western United States.
  1. [Anaheim Solar Energy Plant ](https://www.anaheim.net/5090/Renewable-Energy-Resources#tab8b0957cd-f229-4b8d-b3e0-27c1ff9a9bf7_0)


Anaheim, CA   
  
---  
Completed: August 2014   
  
100% ownership   
  
2.1 MWac   
  
Intermittent generation (weather dependent)  
  
Solar  
  
The City built and owns a 2.1 MW solar photovoltaic system on top of the City-owned Anaheim Convention Center.   
![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=54050)
  1. [Bowerman Power Facility ](https://www.anaheim.net/5090/Renewable-Energy-Resources#tab8b0957cd-f229-4b8d-b3e0-27c1ff9a9bf7_1)


Irvine, CA   
  
---  
Term: May 2016 - April 2036 (20 years)  
  
100% participant share  
  
19.6 MW capacity   
  
Continuous generation (24/7)  
  
Biomethane   
  
The City executed a Power Purchase Agreement with Bowerman Power, LLC to purchase 19.6 MW of renewable energy generated from landfill gas from the Frank R. Bowerman Landfill in Irvine, California.  
![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=54040)
  1. [Brea Power II ](https://www.anaheim.net/5090/Renewable-Energy-Resources#tab8b0957cd-f229-4b8d-b3e0-27c1ff9a9bf7_2)


Brea, CA   
  
---  
Term ends: October 2045   
  
100% participant share  
  
27 MW capacity   
  
Continuous generation (24/7)  
  
Biomethane   
  
The City executed a Power Purchase Agreement with Brea Power Partners, LP to deliver renewable, landfill gas energy to Anaheim Public Utilities.  
  
The original 5 MW contract was replaced with a second long-term contract for a total of 27 MW from the new unit at the Olinda Landfill project.  
![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=54041)
  1. [Canyon Power Plant Solar](https://www.anaheim.net/5090/Renewable-Energy-Resources#tab8b0957cd-f229-4b8d-b3e0-27c1ff9a9bf7_3)


Anaheim, CA   
---  
Completed: January 2012   
  
100% ownership   
  
100 kW capacity   
Intermittent generation (weather dependent)  
  
Solar  
  
The City installed and owns 100 kW of solar at the Canyon Power Plant to offset a portion of the electricity needs of the operations building.  
![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=54042)
  1. [Desert Harvest II​](https://www.anaheim.net/5090/Renewable-Energy-Resources#tab8b0957cd-f229-4b8d-b3e0-27c1ff9a9bf7_4)


Desert Center, CA   
  
---  
Term: December 2020 - November 2045 (25 years)  
  
51% participant share | 36 MW   
  
SCPPA Contract Capacity: 70 MW   
  
Intermittent generation (weather dependent)  
Solar  
The Cities of Anaheim, Burbank, and Vernon have contracted with Desert Harvest II, LLC through Southern California Public Power Authority for a share of intermittent solar energy from one of two solar projects: the (150 MW) Desert Harvest project, or the (500 MW) Maverick project. The City of Anaheim’s share is 36 MW.   
  
![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=54051)
  1. [Grace Solar](https://www.anaheim.net/5090/Renewable-Energy-Resources#tab8b0957cd-f229-4b8d-b3e0-27c1ff9a9bf7_5)


Blythe, CA  
  
---  
Term: NTL* June 2028 – NTL June 2048 (20 years)  
  
59% participant share  
  
100 MW capacity  
  
Intermittent generation (weather dependent)  
  
Solar  
  
The Cities of Anaheim, Pasadena, and Colton have contracted with NextEra Energy Resources Development, LLC through the Southern California Public Power Authority for a share of intermittent solar energy from the Grace Solar Project. The City of Anaheim’s share is 100 MW.  
  
* No Later Than  
![thumbnail_nextera solar grace](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=67030)
  1. [Haypress Hydro Project ](https://www.anaheim.net/5090/Renewable-Energy-Resources#tab8b0957cd-f229-4b8d-b3e0-27c1ff9a9bf7_6)


Sierra County, CA

100% participant share

Hydroelectric   
  
The City has purchased 12.5 MW of intermittent hydroelectric energy from EIF Haypress, LLC. The City receives and pays for energy only when the units are operating.

![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=54044)
  1. [Solar for Schools ](https://www.anaheim.net/5090/Renewable-Energy-Resources#tab8b0957cd-f229-4b8d-b3e0-27c1ff9a9bf7_10)


​Anaheim, CA ​  
  
---  
Completed: February 2019   
  
100% ownership   
1,543 kW capacity  
Intermittent generation (weather dependent)  
  
Solar  
  
The Anaheim Public Utilities teamed up with the Placentia Yorba Linda School District, the Anaheim Elementary School District, the Magnolia School District, the Centralia Elementary School District, and the Savana School District to locate nine solar carports at local public school campuses in Anaheim to educate students on the benefits of solar and help offset the districts' expenses.   
  
The 20-year License Agreements expire in 2039.  
![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=54038)

  6. Educational Partners 


#  Educational Partners 
In partnership with widely recognized experts in the field of education and sustainability, Anaheim Public Utilities provides additional resources and tools for Anaheim students to reinforce their learning through hands-on, engaging and fun activities. We offer exciting and unique learning platforms that contribute to a well-rounded educational experience for our students.
![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=54223)
Through a partnership with Municipal Water District of Orange County (MWDOC), we provide grade-specific water education classes, field trips, and teacher workshops through the Shows that Teach and Inside the Outdoors.  
  
The curriculum for K-2nd graders offers informative assemblies that use music, humor, and audience participation to engage students in water-centric topics such as the water cycle, water supply resources, and conservation. Orange County Department of Education’s Inside the Outdoors offers interactive lessons for students in grades 3-5 that include valuable instruction on the history of California water, local climate and water sources, and how to use water efficiently.
For more information, email [incentives@anaheim.net](https://www.anaheim.net/5227/Incentives@anaheim.net) or call (714) 765-4423.
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

![STOC skyline graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=48628)
# State of the City
Anaheim Mayor Ashleigh Aitken held her inaugural State of the City address and luncheon on May 23, 2023, at City National Grove of Anaheim.
Centered around the theme of "We Are Anaheim," Mayor Aitken talked about how Anaheim neighborhoods are seeing renewal and a focus on services touching the daily lives of residents as the city’s record economic recovery continues with major investment planned in years to come.
You can read Mayor Aitken's State of the City remarks below and watch an archived livestream of the event below.
The 2023 State of the City was a benefit supporting a stronger Anaheim with proceeds going to the Anaheim Community Foundation, a nonprofit that supports an array of programs, services and other nonprofits, all to enrich the lives of Anaheim residents and our community.
[![Read Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=49318)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/49283/Mayor-Aitken-State-of-the-City-5-23-23)
[![Watch Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=49316)](https://reflect-city-of-anaheim.cablecast.tv/CablecastPublicSite/show/176?channel=1)

#  Building Division 
![Building](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=7037)
Welcome to the home page for the Building Division of the City of Anaheim. Our team of professional engineers, inspectors, and friendly support staff is dedicated to providing the highest level of plan review and inspection services to our community. The goal of our staff is to help our customers build safe and sustainable buildings that comply with applicable codes and regulations.
Please contact our division directly at (714) 765-5153 or select one of the links below to learn more.
[![Planning page graphic Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=40201)](https://aca-prod.accela.com/anaheim/default.aspx)
[![EPR Button Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=35415)](https://www.anaheim.net/5758/Building-Division---Electronic-Plan-Revi)
**The Building Division front counter will be under construction from February 26, 2026, through March 3, 2026.  
There may be delays in service during this time. We appreciate your patience and understanding.**
Counter Hours
Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday  
8:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.  
  
Thursday  
Delayed opening 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.
  

Phone Hours
Monday - Friday
8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.
**Please arrive no later than 3 p.m.****to ensure permit and plan check submittals can be completed.**

#  Budget 
The Budget Division oversees the administration of the City's annual budget and collection of revenues due to the city with the following objectives:  

  * Administer the City's budget through monthly review and analysis of city revenues and expenditures
  * Develop and report revenue projections
  * Produce a balanced budget in accordance with city, State and Federal requirements
  * Provide oversight of the City's 5-year Capital Improvement Plan (CIP)
  * Provide oversight to the City's Enterprise, Internal Service, Capital Projects and other Special Revenue funds to ensure that revenues and expenditures are consistent with the budget and that all fund balances are maintained as projected
  * Review budget proposals for city programs and make recommendations to the City Manager


For more information on the city's budget, [click here](http://www.anaheim.net/mycitybudget).
### Contact Us

200 S Anaheim Boulevard  
6th Floor  
Anaheim, CA 92805

Fax: 714-765-5260[![phone icon](https://www.anaheim.net/543/Budget)](https://www.anaheim.net/543/Budget "Call: 714-765-5260")

#  Public Works Records 
To search for Street Improvement, Storm Drain, Sewer and Grading plans, please go to the Public Works Laserfiche [database](http://records.anaheim.net/CityClerk/Welcome.aspx). Cookies must be enabled. If you are unfamiliar with Laserfiche, you may find these [instructions ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5842)useful.  
  
If you still need assistance or if you are looking for soils or geotechnical reports, contact Public Works Records at 714-765-5179 or Email  
  
  

###  County Records  

If you are seeking records on Tract Maps, Parcel Maps, Lot Line Adjustment (LLA), Centerline Ties(Corner Records), and Certificates of Compliance you will need to go to the Orange County Public Works website, [here ](http://ocpublicworks.com/survey/products/records "County Records")or [here](http://landrecords.ocpublicworks.com/ocsl/ "County Land Records"). To contact Orange County Public Works records, please call 714-967-0852.

  6. Power Outage Preparedness


#  Power Outage Preparedness 
A number of things can cause a power outage. While the majority of outages are short term, an unexpected major disaster can cause your home to be without power for an extended period of time. Below is some helpful information you may want to keep handy to ensure that you and your family are prepared to be without power.  
  

  * Keep a kit on hand with the following items, making sure to check your kit and refresh batteries periodically:
    * Flashlight with extra batteries
    * Portable, battery-powered radio or TV
    * Tool box with screwdrivers, wrench, and pliers Work gloves
    * Drinking water (at least 1 gallon per person, per day)
    * List of contacts, including emergency services, physicians, family, and friends 
  * Use a flashlight for emergency lighting—candles can be a serious fire hazard
  * Turn off major appliances, such as air conditioning or pool pumps, to avoid a drain or power surge when the electricity comes back on
  * Leave one light turned on so you know when the power comes back on
  * Avoid opening your refrigerator or freezer to keep contents cold
  * Do not run a generator inside an enclosed area, and open your main circuit breaker—generation can backfeed into the utility grid and cause damage to equipment
  * Do not place electrical cords so that they run through doorways or under carpets
  * Check that the wattage of all bulbs in light fixtures is the correct wattage for your fixtures
  * Keep space heaters three feet away from any combustible materials such as bedding, clothing, and draperies
  * If you are in your vehicle, remember to treat flashing signals as stop signs


If you are outside and power is interrupted (or lost), remember to exercise extreme caution around any downed power lines. Never touch or approach the wires or utility pole – report any down wires immediately to emergency personnel. If you do come across downed power lines, make sure to follow these steps:  

  1. Stay inside until help arrives.
  2. Do not grab or lean against vehicles, poles or other objects in the vicinity of the power lines.
  3. Take small shuffle steps away from the area with your feet and legs close together.
  4. Do not touch anyone or any other object until you are well away from the scene.

![Preparing for wildfire header](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=33879)

and resources you need to get started.

  5. Transient Occupancy Tax


#  Transient Occupancy Tax 
For the privilege of occupancy in the City of Anaheim, each transient is subject to and shall pay a transient occupancy tax of 15% of the rent charged by the operator. The 15% tax MUST be separately stated from rent pursuant to Chapter 2.12 Subsection 2.12.020.010 of the Anaheim Municipal Code.
  

For more information, please refer to the [Transient Occupancy Tax Ordinance](https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/anaheim/latest/anaheim_ca/0-0-0-51668) (Chapter 2.12) for detailed information.
  

If you should have a change in your operation, or a change in controllers or staff who prepare the monthly TOT documents, please do not hesitate contacting the Business License staff with any questions at (714) 765-5194. This is a great complimentary service to help avoid audit findings.
### **TOT Collection**
Please ensure your payment along with your TOT Return Notice is received in our office no later than 5 p.m. on the last city business day of the month. If you plan on walking the payment into City Hall please note our modified hours. 
  * **For Overnight/Express Delivery Only** : City of Anaheim, License Collector, 200 S. Anaheim Blvd., #136 Anaheim, CA 92805
  * **Mail** : City of Anaheim, License Collector, P.O. Box 61042, Anaheim, CA 92803-6142


**Public Counter Hours: 8 a.m. - 3 p.m.**

  4. Information Services


#  Information Services 
  * Manages the acquisition, maintenance and security of all of the city’s Information Services assets including all network infrastructure, system servers, enterprise data, personal computers, and telecommunications equipment.
  * Provides Information Services support for all departments in the city.
  * Responsible for maintaining continuous operations of the city’s network infrastructure as well as availability of all core systems.
  * Maintains the City’s IT Service Desk, providing user support for hardware and software requests/issues. 


### Contact Us
  1. ### Information Services Division
200 S Anaheim Boulevard  
Ste. 643  
Anaheim, California 92805
**City IT Management**   
Phone: 714-765-5046[![phone icon](https://www.anaheim.net/591/Information-Services)](https://www.anaheim.net/591/Information-Services "Call: 714-765-5046")   
Fax: 714-765-5260[![phone icon](https://www.anaheim.net/591/Information-Services)](https://www.anaheim.net/591/Information-Services "Call: 714-765-5260")  
Infoservices@anaheim.net
### Hours
Monday - Friday  
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.

  4. Community Care Facilities


![Community Care Facilities header](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34831)
Anaheim regulates **community care facilities** and **sober living home** s to ensure supportive living spaces for those with disabilities and those recovering from addiction, while also preserving the character of our neighborhoods.
The city regulates community care facilities and sober living homes through **permits, registration, location and operational standards**.
Community care facilities and sober living homes with six or fewer people and not licensed by the state of California must get either a **city operator's permit** or **operator's registration**.
**Large facilities** , with seven or more people, and whether they are licensed by the state or not, must get a city **conditional-use permit** allowing for and outlining conditions for operation.
All community care facilities and sober living homes with six or fewer people and not licensed by the state are required to **apply for a permit or register by April 27, 2021** , and **comply with regulations and operating standards by Oct. 29, 2021**.
Just below, you can read an overview fact sheet, access a fillable application and see Anaheim's ordinance regulating community care facilities.
[![Fact sheet button Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34877)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/34882/Community-Care-Facilities-fact-sheet-fall-2020)
[![Apply button Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34876)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/34519/Z213---Community-Care-Facility-Unlicensed-and-Sober-Living-Home-Application)
[![Ordinance button Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34878)](http://records.anaheim.net/CityClerk/DocView.aspx?id=2222468&dbid=0&repo=CITYOFANAHEIM)

  5. Project ViewFinder


#  Project ViewFinder 
## Welcome to Project ViewFinder!
  
Project ViewFinder gives a glimpse into Anaheim's future. As one of the largest cities in the United States, with more than 345,000 residents and 20 million visitors a year. Anaheim is leading the way in providing up-to-date information about the city's plans. Project ViewFinder is Anaheim's latest mapping tool in helping keep residents. businesses. developers, contractors and utility companies informed about Anaheim's many current and upcoming projects.   
  

## Residents
  
You can use Project ViewFinder to learn more about the construction projects and improvements taking place in your neighborhood or along your commute.   
  

## Business Professionals
  
Use Project ViewFinder to track Anaheim's future and current construction projects. as well as find out how you can partner with the City in providing high quality, cost effective services in the Anaheim community.   
  

## How it Works
  
To use Project ViewFinder select the type of project you'd like to see by its icon or type in an address or intersection in the search bar. Just hover your cursor over any of the icons on the map to find project details and who to contact for additional information for each project.   

  * [Launch Project Viewfinder](https://gis.anaheim.net/ProjectViewFinder)

  6. Air Purifiers - For Schools


#  Air Purifiers - For School 
**
### Anaheim Public Utilities offers K-12 public and private schools rebates for room air cleaners, also known as air purifier.  

  
**
Air purifiers are portable appliances that remove fine particles, such as pollen, dust, and unpleasant odors from the air. Using ENERGY STAR® portable air purifiers is a great way to improve indoor air quality in a single room or area while using energy efficiently.
  * ENERGY STAR® certified air purifier: $75 per unit (maximum incentive up to $25,000)
  * For complete rebate [program guidelines, W-9 and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/36232/Schools-Air-Purifier).


For more information about ENERGY STAR® certified room air purifiers and a list of certified models, visit [energystar.gov/productfinder](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-room-air-cleaners/results).
  

## Helpful Resources:
  

[ENERGY STAR®](https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/air_purifiers_cleaners)
[Environmental Protection Agency](https://www.epa.gov/coronavirus/air-cleaners-hvac-filters-and-coronavirus-covid-19)

  4. [Building Division](https://www.anaheim.net/5252/Building-Division)
  5. Permit Processing Info


#  Permit Processing Info 
Building Division staff reviews construction plans to verify compliance with State Building Codes and local regulations. We are currently under the provisions of the 2025 Edition of the California Building Standards Codes Cycle.
If you are wondering if you need a Building Permit for your proposed project, please see the following PDF for an detailed explanation of when a permit is required:  [When Is A Permit Required?](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/41215)
To submit a project for plan review, please "Plans Required for Submittal" handout below to determine the number of plan sets required based on your scope of work.
[Plans Required for Submittal](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/41216)
We will also need a completed Plan Check Application form. All forms can be found at the following link: 
[Building Permit Forms, Applications & Fees](https://www.anaheim.net/6013/Forms-Applications-and-Fees)
When submitting for a second cycle of plan review, prior to issuance, this is referred to as a resubmittal (Not a revision). Each subsequent submittal is called a resubmittal. DO NOT cloud the changes made to the plans. Please provide a narrative identifying the changes. Re-submittals of plans due to plan check comments will require:
  1. Old Plan Check Sets
  2. New Corrected Construction Sets
  3. Plan Check Comment Response Letter
  4. DO NOT CLOUD CORRECTIONS


If the plans have been approved, the permit issued, and a change to the plan is required. This is called a revision. All changes to the plans MUST be clouded. If your project needs a revision, please see the following document for steps and procedures.
[Plan Revision Procedure](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/39718)
* * *
# Construction Plan Requirements
Detailed construction plans are required for most types of projects. Construction plans must clearly indicate the location, nature, and extent of the project, and show in detail how it will comply with the Building Codes.
For a permit application to be deemed complete for submittal, the submittal documents must contain all information as required per California Building Code Section 107.
**This includes, but not be limited to:**
## **Architectural Plans***
  * Site Plan showing Property Line Boundaries, All Structures on Property and Project Location
  * Detailed and Dimensioned Floor and Roof Plans
  * Detailed and Dimensioned Building Elevations and Cross-Sections
  * Means of Egress Analysis
  * Green Building Code Requirements
  * Title 24 Energy Calculations


## **Structural Plans***
  * Foundation Plan
  * Framing Plans
  * Roof Framing Plans
  * Structural Details
  * Structural Calculations


## Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing and Landscaping Plans (as necessary)
*Please be advised that not all items may be necessary based on the proposed scope of work. Please speak with a Plan Check Engineer with the Building Division to clarify if an item is necessary for submittal.
* * *
# **Getting Construction Plans Prepared for Your Project**
Construction plans must be prepared by a qualified person. Building & Safety staff does not prepare plans. For most project types, the plans must be prepared by a California Licensed Architect or Engineer.
For help hiring an Architect, please download the [Consumer's Guide to Hiring an Architect](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/39682).
**Submitting incomplete, inaccurate, or non-compliant plans could delay the permit process for your project and incur additional plan review fees.**
* * *
# **Over-the-Counter Plan Review**
Smaller projects which require no more than 30 minutes for plan review can typically be reviewed over-the-counter. Depending on the complexity and scope of your project, or the existing conditions which may be present, Building & Safety may require that you submit your plans for a more detailed review. The following types of projects are normally reviewed over-the-counter:
  * Residential Patio Covers
  * Swimming Pools
  * Wall Signs
  * Re-roofing

#  Student Success Portal 
![StudentSuccess_Web Banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38508)
Anaheim Public Library (APL) and local school districts are working together to make it easy for students and staff to access tutoring, eBooks, language learning, test prep resources, and more for free on Anaheim Public Library's Student Success portal. 
Click the buttons below to enter the Student Success School District portals.

  4. Purchasing


#  Purchasing 
The Purchasing Division contracts for a wide variety of materials, equipment and operating services for all city departments. For purchases over $50,000, competitive requests for bids or proposals are issued through the city’s electronic bid management system, bid results are posted online, and orders are awarded to the lowest responsive and responsible bidder. Multiple quotations are solicitated from registered vendors for purchases between $10,000 and $50,000.
Purchasing is also responsible for the disposal of the city’s obsolete or surplus equipment and scrap materials. The division adheres to the Standards of Purchasing Practice of the California Association of Public Purchasing Officers to ensure that suppliers are treated fairly and the city’s interests are protected. The city welcomes new suppliers and encourages businesses to register for electronic notifications of our bid opportunities.
## Ethics Policy
The Purchasing Division strives to ensure that all individuals and businesses are treated professionally and fairly, without bias, prejudice or favoritism. As such, all Purchasing Division staff and any other City employees involved in the procurement process and interacting with the City’s suppliers are expected to adhere to the ethical guidelines contained in the City’s Administrative Regulation 130: Statement of Policy on Fraud, as well as the California Association of Public Purchasing Officers “Standards of Purchasing Practice” embedded herein. Ethics violations include, but are not limited to accepting anything of material value from any current or potential supplier, disclosing confidential or proprietary information to suppliers and/or their competitors, altering bid or contract documents, or establishing specifications in such a way as to discourage competitive bids or specifically exclude suppliers to give unfair advantage to others. 
**CALIFORNIA ASSOCIATION OF PUBLIC PURCHASING OFFICERS**
Standards of Purchasing Practice: 
  * To regard public service as a sacred trust, giving primary consideration to the interests of the public agency that employs us. 
  * To purchase without prejudice, seeking to obtain the maximum value for each dollar expended. 
  * To avoid unfair practices, giving all qualified vendors equal opportunity. 
  * To honor our obligations and require that obligations to our public agency be honored. 
  * To accord vendor representatives courteous treatment, remembering that these representatives are important sources of information and assistance in solving our purchasing needs. 
  * To refuse to accept any form of commercial bribery, and prevent any appearance of so doing. 
  * To be receptive to counsel from our colleagues, and to cooperate with them to promote a spirit of teamwork and unity. 
  * To conduct ourselves with fairness and dignity, and to demand honesty and truth in the purchasing process. 
  * To strive for greater knowledge of purchasing methods and of the materials we purchase. 
  * To cooperate with all organizations and individuals involved in activities designed to enhance the development of the purchasing profession, remembering that our actions reflect on the entire purchasing profession.

  6. Military Equipment Policy


#  Military Equipment Policy 
## What is the Military Equipment Funding, Acquisition and Use Policy?
Effective Jan. 1, 2022, California state law under Assembly Bill 481 requires law enforcement agencies, including the Anaheim Police Department, to obtain approval from the Anaheim City Council, by adoption of a military equipment use policy, prior to taking certain actions relating to the funding, acquisition, or use of military equipment. 
You can read more about California Assembly Bill 481 [here](https://gcc02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fleginfo.legislature.ca.gov%2Ffaces%2FbillNavClient.xhtml%3Fbill_id%3D202120220AB481&data=04%7C01%7CERyan%40anaheim.net%7C3bc2fc8dd46447f8df1008da17e1c845%7C74c3739c502a49c68d212bbc30f56f22%7C0%7C0%7C637848555733498003%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000&sdata=KVZa0EM3ksFuHf5%2FV48RfOU6wlMolGSjXatOeatmEbw%3D&reserved=0).
As you read through this page, you’ll see the law's definition of military equipment is broad and includes common police equipment not sourced from the military. 
## Definition of Military Equipment
California state law designates items in the following categories as military equipment:
  * Unmanned, remotely piloted, powered aerial or ground vehicles
  * Mine-resistant ambush-protected (MRAP) vehicles, such as armored cars
  * High mobility multipurpose wheeled vehicles, commonly referred to as Humvees, two and one-half-ton trucks, five-ton trucks, or wheeled vehicles that have a breaching or entry apparatus attached Excluded from this group: unarmored all-terrain vehicles (ATVs) and motorized dirt bikes. 
  * Tracked armored vehicles that provide ballistic protection to occupants 
  * Command and control vehicles that are either built or modified to facilitate the operational control and direction of public safety units
  * Weaponized aircraft, vessels, or vehicles of any kind
  * Battering rams, slugs, and breaching apparatuses that are explosive in nature; this excludes a handheld, one-person ram
  * Firearms and ammunition of .50 caliber or greater; excluding standard issue shotguns and related ammunition
  * Specialized firearms and ammunition of less than .50 caliber, including assault weapons as defined in Sections 30510 and 30515 of the Penal Code, with the exception of standard issue service weapons and ammunition
  * Any firearm or firearm accessory that is designed to launch explosive projectiles
  * Noise-flash diversionary devices and explosive breaching tools
  * Munitions containing tear gas or OC; excluding standard, service-issued handheld pepper spray
  * TASER Shockwave, microwave weapons, water cannons, and the Long Range Acoustic Devices (LRADs)
  * Kinetic energy weapons and munitions, including specialty impact munition (SIM) weapons 
  * Any other equipment as determined by a governing body or a state agency to require additional oversight


## Requirements
State law requires each law enforcement agency's governing body to adopt a written military use policy by ordinance in addition to other requirements by the bill. The following are required:
  * Publish the draft military equipment use policy to the police department’s website 30 days before a public hearing to approve the policy
  * Obtain approval by the city council, by adoption of a military equipment use policy, prior to taking certain actions relating to the funding, acquisition, or use of military equipment, as defined above
  * Publish an annual report to include each type of military equipment approved by the city council
  * Annual review of the military equipment use ordinance by the city council, with the option to either disapprove the renewal of a type of military equipment or amend the military equipment use policy if it determines that the military equipment does not comply with standards for approval
  * Hold at least one well publicized and conveniently located community engagement meeting within 30 days of submitting and publicly releasing the annual military equipment report


## Annual Report
Following approval of a military equipment use policy, the police department is required to submit to the city council an annual military equipment report for each type of approved military equipment.
The 2023 Military Equipment Annual Report can be found [here](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57377/2023-Military-Equipment-Report).
### **Additional Requirements for the Report**
The annual military equipment report shall at a minimum, include the following information for the immediately preceding calendar year for each type of military equipment:
  * A summary of how the military equipment was used and the purpose of its use
  * A summary of any complaints or concerns received concerning the military equipment
  * The results of any internal audits, any information about violations of the military equipment use policy, and any actions taken in response
  * The total annual cost for each type of military equipment, including acquisition, personnel, training, transportation, maintenance, storage, upgrade, and other ongoing costs; and from what source funds will be provided for the military equipment in the calendar year following submission of the annual military equipment report
  * The quantity possessed for each type of military equipment
  * If the law enforcement agency intends to acquire additional military equipment in the next year, the quantity sought for each type of military equipment
  * Hold at least one well publicized and conveniently located community engagement meeting within 30 days of submitting and publicly releasing the annual military equipment report


## Policy, presentations
  * [2024-2025 Military Equipment Policy](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57655/24---25-Military-Equipment-Policy-Section-705)
  * [2023-2024 Military Equipment Policy](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/51312/DRAFT-2023-Military-Equipment-Policy)
  * [Official policy, approved May 2022](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/44283/Military-Equipment-Policy-May-2022)
  * [March 24, 2022 Police Review Board presentation](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/43268/AB-481---Presentation)


## **Public Meetings**
### **Upcoming**
  

### **Past**
  * **November 19, 2024:** Anaheim City Council approves policy; [click here](https://www.anaheim.net/6216/Military-Equipment-Policy) to watch.
  * **June 27, 2024:**[Police Review Board meeting presentation](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55792/AB-481-PRB-Presentation---PRB-Update-2024)
  * **July 20, 2023:**[Police Review Board meeting presentation](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50063/AB-481-PRB-Presentation---PRB-2023)**  
**
  * **May 24, 2022: Anaheim City Council holds public hearing, approves policy; **[**click here**](http://anaheim.granicus.com/player/clip/2829?view_id=2&redirect=true)**to watch**
  * **April 26, 2022:** Anaheim City Council holds first discussion on the new policy, [click here](https://anaheim.granicus.com/player/clip/2816?view_id=2&redirect=true) to watch
  * **March 24, 2022:**[Police Review Board meeting presentation](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/43268/AB-481---Presentation)


## Questions, concerns or complaints 
The Anaheim Police Department is committed to providing professional, quality service to our community. Any member of the public can register a question, concern, or complaining regarding military equipment used by the Anaheim Police Department. 
If you would like to file a formal complaint, please use the personnel complaint form[ here ](http://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Police-Department-13/Customer-Service-Personnel-Complaint-Eng-84)(Spanish form [here](http://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Police-Department-13/Customer-Service-Quejas-Contra-El-Person-92)). Hard copies of the form are available at any Anaheim police station, the City Clerk's office, Anaheim Public Library branches or the Community Services office.
Questions or concerns can be submitted online through Anaheim Anytime via the MyAnaheim app or online [here](http://www.anaheim.net/2128/Anaheim-Anytime).

#  Electric Vehicles 
### Anaheim Public Utilities offers EV rebates and programs to support our customer's path towards electrification. Whether you are new to the process or need hands-on support with your site plans, we're here to help you every step of the way. 
###   

### For more information about Anaheim Public Utilities' future adoption of EV’s in our community, please review our [Integrated Resources Plan](https://www.anaheim.net/4864) and [Southern California Association of Governments study](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50587/APU-Southern-California-Association-of-Governments-study).
###   

### Contact us by email or call 714-765-4250.

  1. [Home](https://www.anaheim.net/)
  2. [Departments](https://www.anaheim.net/148/Departments)
  3. [City Administration](https://www.anaheim.net/149/City-Administration)
  4. [Communications ](https://www.anaheim.net/151/Communications)
  5. City of Kindness


![kindness](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=14494)
City of Kindness is Anaheim's adopted motto and a way of creating a better quality of life in our city as well as resiliency in the face of natural disasters and other challenges.
kindness is a simple but powerful concept. It seeks to forge a stronger city by connecting people, neighbors and neighborhoods and by building a shared sense of community.
That can have a positive impact on the challenges our city and many others face, including crime, bullying, senior neglect and alcohol and drug abuse.
A city connected through kindness also is more resilient. At its core, kindness is a way of promoting community resiliency and partnership in the face of earthquakes, wildfires and other disasters.
Anaheim's City of Kindness motto has its roots in the tragic loss of a young resident.
Natasha Jaievsky lost her life in a 2002 car accident. After her passing, her family learned how Natasha constantly wrote and talked about kindness, leaving behind drawings and writings about kindness in her bedroom closet and drawers.
The family went on to honor Natasha's legacy with “Make Kindness Contagious” banners displayed around Anaheim.
In 2017, Anaheim's City Council adopted City of Kindness as a motto.

#  Legislative Impacts on Utilities 
Each year, thousands of new bills are introduced as part of the state and federal legislative process, with many having the potential to affect Anaheim Public Utilities’ operations and rates.
Even though Anaheim Public Utilities operates on a not-for-profit basis, many legislative bills still result in increased costs to provide electric service in California. In fact, recent and pending legislative mandates are expected to continue to increase Electric service costs into the future.
However, more and more legislative and regulatory mandates, as well as their associated costs, remain beyond the control of our local policy body. Instead, they are controlled by state and federal policy makers, and even though many of our local Orange County legislators urge their peers to consider the costs when implementing new legislation, many bills are still passed through the legislature that will ultimately raise the cost of both electric and water services.
## Affordable Electric and Water Rates
Anaheim residents still enjoy affordable electric and water rates in comparison to other Orange County cities. Our focus is to maximize customer benefits and to minimize any negative impacts that proposed legislation may have on your rates. Our agency is municipally-owned and our budget and policies are reviewed by the Public Utilities Board and approved by Anaheim City Council.
![CA state capital](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31523)
![CA State Flag Capital](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31522)

  6. Wire Theft and Vandalism


#  Wire Theft and Vandalism 
![theft banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51087)
Wire theft and vandalism are on the rise. This is a serious issue impacting the community and Anaheim Public Utilities needs your help to identify theft and report illegal activity. 
It is important to note that non-operational streetlights may have exposed wires near the base, and nearby electrical box lids may be removed. Those involved in the theft of wire may be wearing construction clothing and operating unbranded vehicles. Beware of suspicious individuals in the act of removing materials and causing damage to the light.
**If you see suspicious activity or a theft in progress, DO NOT ENGAGE! Call 714-765-1900.**
To report damaged or non-working streetlights:
  * Request streetlight repair through the [My Anaheim app](https://www.anaheim.net/1409/My-Anaheim-App)
  * Submit a concern through the [Anaheim Anytime website](https://www.anaheim.net/2128)
  * Call 714-765-3300


We appreciate your patience during this time. Wiring repairs may take several weeks depending on the extent of the damage.
Anaheim Public Utilities is using different methods of reducing theft issues such as solar-powered lighting solutions, tamper-proof electrical boxes, utilizing wires made with non-copper materials, and collaborating with the Anaheim Police Department on theft attempt trends.
  1. ![theft 01](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51093)
  2. ![theft 02](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51094)
  3. ![theft 03](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51095)


  1. [ 1 theft 01 ](https://www.anaheim.net/6463/Wire-Theft-and-Vandalism#targetImage)
  2. [ 2 theft 02 ](https://www.anaheim.net/6463/Wire-Theft-and-Vandalism#targetImage)
  3. [ 3 theft 03 ](https://www.anaheim.net/6463/Wire-Theft-and-Vandalism#targetImage)


## How can you help?  
  

  * Call 714-765-1900 to report theft in progress – **DO NOT ENGAGE**
  * Report street light outages and vandalism through:
    * 714-765-3300
    * [My Anaheim](https://www.anaheim.net/1409/My-Anaheim-App)
    * [Anaheim Anytime](https://www.anaheim.net/2128)


[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)
  1. [Street Lighting Projects](https://www.anaheim.net/3420/Street-Lighting-Project)
  2. [Wire Theft and Vandalism](https://www.anaheim.net/6463/Wire-Theft-and-Vandalism)

  5. GHAD watering tips


#  GHAD watering tips 
## About the Santiago Landslide and creation of the Geologic Hazard Abatement District
In 1993, following intense El Nino rains that started in late 1992, a bluff in east Anaheim slid prompting the evacuation of homes and resulting in property damage, emergency remediation work and litigation.
Since then, a largely unseen system of wells, pumps, drains and meters has helped keep land stable and protect homes in the area of the 1993 landslide.
Following litigation and installation of the wells, pumps and drains, the Santiago Geologic Hazard Abatement District, GHAD for short, was formed to oversee and manage the system. The district is a state entity and independent of the city of Anaheim.
It's anticipated that in early 2024, the GHAD will run out of funding to keep the wells, pumps and drains operational. In anticipation of the pumps not working, its recommended residents limit outdoor watering. 
## Tips to limit outdoor watering
![Turn off or adjust sprinklers](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49895) Turn off or adjust sprinkler controllers
  

  

  

![Water no more than 3 days](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49892) Water no more than 3 days a week _or less_
  

  

  

![low-water irrigation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49893) Install low-water drip irrigation
  

  

  

![Low water landscaping](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49894) Use low-water landscaping _  
_
