#  Comprehensive Energy Assessment 
## Discover ways to optimize your building’s energy usage and save money. A comprehensive energy assessment of your business could be your first step to saving thousands, and ultimately boost your bottom line. Our energy experts will schedule a site visit to help you with your energy efficiency goals.
### Program Advantages 
  

Customers who take advantage of our Comprehensive Energy Assessment Program will receive a customized analysis report, which will identify opportunities to help them use energy more efficiently. Customers can potentially reduce their electric use by as much as 10% to 25% per month by implementing the recommendations.  

### How the Program Works
  

  1. Our Anaheim energy expert will conduct an in-depth energy analysis of your site.
  2. You will be provided an energy assessment report which will highlight energy savings recommendations, analysis of existing energy consumption, proposed efficiency upgrades, energy cost evaluation, estimated installation costs, and applicable incentive amounts.


Energy assessment reports may include detailed analysis of the following measures:
  * Lighting systems
  * HVAC systems
  * Building envelope (insulation, widow tint, cool roofs, etc.)
  * Equipment operation
  * Industrial processes
  * Motors
  * Refrigeration
  * Electric water heating


If you have any questions about the program, simply fill out our [online form](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Energy-Efficiency-Solutions-61). A representative will contact you within 2-3 business days.
### Additional Resources:
  

  * **[Energy.gov Baseline Energy Calculator:](https://scout.energy.gov/baseline-energy-calculator.html%20)**

Provides estimated energy use and carbon dioxide emissions associated with commercial buildings. For informational purposes only.

  1. ## Commercial Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email

#  Programs 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [Community Emergency Response Team (CERT)](https://www.anaheim.net/887/Community-Emergency-Response-Team-CERT)
Anaheim CERT promotes the ongoing training and participation of our volunteer force by providing many educational opportunities to better prepare for and respond to disasters.
  2. ####  [EMT Scholarship Program ](https://www.anaheim.net/6450/EMT-Scholarship-Program)
Anaheim Fire & Rescue, in conjunction with the Anaheim Community Foundation, has established an Emergency Medical Technician (EMT) Scholarship Program for our Anaheim community interested in pursuing a career in Public Safety. 
  3. ####  [Explorer Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5427/Explorer-Program)
Is open to youth between 15-21 years of age, designed to provide hands-on experience for those interested in building Leadership, Teamwork, and Accountability skills, as well as for those considering a career as a firefighter. For more information or to learn when our next quarterly recruitment meeting is scheduled, please call 714-765-4036.
  4. ####  [FireMedics Program](https://www.anaheim.net/615/FireMedics-Program)
The Paramedic Membership Program is an official program offered by Anaheim Fire & Rescue, which offsets the cost of emergency medical responses.
  5. ####  [RACES](https://www.anaheim.net/1209/RACES)
Thank you for your interest in the Anaheim Radio Amateur Civil Emergency Service (RACES) program, sponsored by the Anaheim Fire & Rescue's Office of Disaster Preparedness. 
  6. ####  [Small Hydrocarbon Pick-Up Program ](https://www.anaheim.net/728/Small-Hydrocarbon-Pick-Up-Program)
Read about efficient and effective means of managing and handling certain wastes on public property generated by spills, abandonment and traffic accidents.
  7. ####  [Used Oil & Household Hazardous Waste](https://www.anaheim.net/729/Used-Oil-Household-Hazardous-Waste)
The Anaheim Fire Department’s Community Relations Section and the California Integrated Waste Management Board (CIWMB) maintain a program that promotes the proper management of used oil and household hazardous waste.
  8. ####  [READY, SET, GO!](https://www.anaheim.net/5629/Preparing-for-Wildfire)
Ready, Set, Go! gives you the tools to protect you, your family, property and community from wildfires. Many residents have built their homes and landscaped without fully understanding the impact that could result from wildfire.


[![Anaheim Fire Patch Logo 275px](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44704)](https://www.anaheim.net/6096)

#  Biking & Hiking Trails 
The map below shows the network of riding and hiking trails in East Anaheim. An [expanded view of the map](https://www.google.com/maps/d/viewer?mid=zXn2mM0W2GBU.ksVPzVOlxsqE) is also available. Access the [facilities resource](https://www.anaheim.net/Facilities/Facility/PrepopulatedSearch?isReservableOnly=false&categoryIDs=9) to see details on each individual trail head/park.

DEV2024-00058 Wyndham Hotel Density Modification
  * [Initial Study](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/66808/)
  * [Appendix A - Air Quality and Greenhouse gas Analysis](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/66812/)
  * [Appendix B - Traffic Circulation Study](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/66811/)
  * [Appendix C - Noise Data](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/66810/)
  * [Appendix D - Native American Tribal Consultation Letters](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/66809/)
  * [Notice of Intent to Adopt a Mitigated Negative Declaration and Notice of Public Hearing](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/67333/)

|  **PROJECT LOCATION:** 515 West Katella Avenue. The approximately 0.97-acre project site (APN 082-271-05) is at 515 West Katella Avenue in the central part of the City of Anaheim in Orange County. Regional access to the project site is from Interstate 5 (I-5), approximately 0.6 mile to the east via West Katella Avenue, and from State Route 22 (SR-22), approximately 2.5 miles to the south via South Harbor Boulevard. **PROJECT DESCRPTION:** The applicant is proposing a General Plan Amendment and Specific Plan Amendment to the Anaheim Resort Specific Plan, to create a new density category for the project site, “Medium Density (Modified B)” to allow for up to 107 hotel rooms and 6,394 square feet of accessory uses. The original Wyndham Hotel building plans were designed with several dual suites that included two bedrooms and two bathrooms with internal connecting doors and one keyed entry door. Following the City’s approval of the project, additional entry doors were added to the dual suite rooms to create two separate keyed rooms. Some dual suites also had their interior connecting doors removed. This resulted in an increase in the total number of rooms from 72 to 107. Changes were also made to the use of space on the first floor without the addition of any square footage. Space that was previously approved for lobby guest areas, storage, and offices were converted to additional conference and meeting rooms and a restaurant that is open to the public (an increase in 6,394 SF of accessory structures). These were all changes made to the interior of the building; the overall development footprint of the building and remainder of the site did not change.    
|  Amanda Lauffer Phone: 714-765-4479

[Notice of Availability (NOA)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/65765/) |  **PROJECT LOCATION:** The project site is located along the Santa Ana River corridor, generally extending from Orangewood Avenue north to Ball Road and the existing Anaheim Coves at Burris Basin. It generally encompasses approximately a 2-mile stretch of the Santa Ana River, covering approximately 111 acres. It is approximately 15 miles upstream from the Pacific Ocean and adjacent to Angel Stadium, Anaheim Regional Transportation Intermodal Center (ARTIC), the Honda Center, and the Arena Corporate Center (ACC) buildings on the west side in the City of Anaheim. The ARTIC, the Honda Center, and the ACC buildings are all part of the OCVIBE Master Site Plan Project (OCVIBE Project). On the east side of the Santa Ana River, in the City of Orange, the project is near the Sandra Hutchens Regional Law Enforcement Training Center (a facility that includes a combat shooting range), the Orange County Public Works offices, the Stadium Promenade shopping mall, the Extended Stay America – Orange, and the Kinder Morgan Orange Terminal. **PROJECT DESCRIPTION:** The OC River Walk Conceptual Master Plan consists of various multi-purpose improvements to transform the Santa Ana River corridor and portions of the existing Santa Ana River Trail (SART), along with related off-site improvements necessary for its implementation. The main components of the Conceptual Master Plan involve creating two water impoundments by installing three inflatable rubber dams, modifying the riverbanks, constructing two pedestrian/bike bridges, providing new trails, improving existing trails, creating new and protection of existing access ramps, constructing two new undercrossings on the west bank at the Union Pacific Railroad and the Metrolink railroad, providing enhanced community recreation amenities (e.g., terraced seating, nature play areas, parklets, plazas, gathering areas for large-scale events, festivals, and other activities, water access, kayak launch, etc.), and other improvements. ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/65765/) |  Ana Straabe

[Festival Anaheim Hills DEIR Planning Commission Workshop](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/65697/) (July 14, 2025) |  **PROJECT LOCATION:** The project site is in the Anaheim Hills area of the City in Orange County, California. The Anaheim Hills area is predominately residential with commercial, and office uses along major corridors. The project site is the existing 85.7-acre Anaheim Hills Festival Specific Plan (Specific Plan), which includes the following Assessor Parcel Numbers (APNs): 354-451-01, -03, -04, -06, -08, -10, -12, -13, -17, -19, -20, -24, -27, -28, -31, -32, -33, -34, -35, -37, and -38. The Specific Plan, inclusive of the project’s development area, is located south of State Route 91 (SR 91) and is generally located south of Santa Ana Canyon Road, and west of Roosevelt Road. **PROJECT DESCRIPTION:** The project site’s proposed development area falls within the newly created DA 5 for Mixed-Use purposes. The development area is limited to DA 5, which includes the following APNs: 354-451-19 and -32. The exterior boundaries of the Specific Plan area would not change. The proposed development would demolish the existing approximately 62,676 square foot theater and, in its place, allow for the development of a 447-unit multi-family rental residential community. The proposed multi-family rental residential community would consist of a four-story multi-family residential building wrapped around a five-level parking structure with one subterranean level. As part of the project’s requested entitlements, a General Plan Amendment and Specific Plan Amendment would be required to allow for the proposed residential uses and for the creation of the new DA 5. The General Plan Amendment would change the land use designation from Regional Commercial to the Mixed-Use Medium for DA 5. The Specific Plan Amendment would amend the current Specific Plan to allow for mixed-use land uses on a total of 16.2 acres (DA 5) and would include other clarifying text amendments related to signage, permitted uses, etc. |  Amanda Lauffer   
Phone: 714-765-4479

|  **PROJET LOCATION:** The proposed project is a Citywide project. Located in northeastern Orange County, the City of Anaheim and its sphere of influence lie approximately 35 miles southeast of downtown Los Angeles and 7 miles north of Santa Ana. The City is surrounded by the cities of Fullerton, Placentia, and Yorba Linda to the north; Riverside County to the east; the cities of Orange, Garden Grove, and Stanton and unincorporated Orange County to the south; and the cities of Cypress and Buena Park to the west. The City encompasses over 34,000 acres of land, stretching nearly 20 miles along the Riverside Freeway (SR-91), and includes another 2,431 acres of unincorporated land in its sphere of influence. In addition to SR-91, regional access to and from Anaheim is provided by the Santa Ana (I-5), Orange (SR-57), and Costa Mesa (SR-55) Freeways; the Eastern Transportation Corridor (SR-241); and Amtrak and Metrolink passenger train services at Angel Stadium and Anaheim Canyon Stations. **PROJECT DESCRIPTION:** The proposed project is a focused update of the City of Anaheim’s General Plan. The General Plan is a State-required legal document that provides guidance to decision-makers regarding the allocation of resources and determining the future physical form and character of development in the city. It is the official statement regarding the extent and types of development needed to achieve the community’s physical, economic, social, and environmental goals. Although the General Plan is composed of individual sections, or “elements” that individually address a specific area of concern, the General Plan embodies a comprehensive and integrated planning approach for the jurisdiction.  Under State law, each city and county general plan has eight mandated elements, including land use, circulation, housing, conservation, open space, noise and safety, and environmental justice. These elements can be formatted in any manner within a general plan and do not need to be “stand-alone” chapters. Government Code Section 65303 also permits local jurisdictions to formulate other elements, which, in the “judgment of the planning agency,” relate to the physical development of the jurisdiction. These “permissive” elements are as legally binding as a mandatory element, once adopted. The City of Anaheim has directed the inclusion of an additional permissive element in its currently adopted General Plan, economic development.   
|  Nick Taylor   
Phone: 714-765-4323

](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57610/)[Appendix K - Shared Parking Analysis Update](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57611/) |  **PROJECT LOCATION:** The approximately 100-acre OCVIBE project site is generally bounded by State Route 57 (SR-57) to the west (excluding the parcel at the southwest corner of Katella Avenue and Douglass Road), the Santa Ana River to the east, the confluence of the SR-57 and Santa Ana River to the south, and the Southern California Edison easement to the north. Off-site improvement areas include the State Route 57 (SR-57) northbound Katella Avenue off-ramp and intersection, SR-57 southbound Ball Road off-ramp and intersection, and dedication and improvements to River Road. **PROJECT DESCRIPTION:** The project proposes amendments to the previously-approved OCVIBE mixed-use project (DEV2020-00125) in the Platinum Triangle to demolish the Arena Corporate Center office buildings and construct up to 750 additional residential units including affordable units with a density bonus and up to 325,000 square feet (s.f.) of office space; potentially remove the previously-approved pedestrian bridge from Parking Deck C across Douglass Road; remove the approved Meadow Park Amphitheater; and dedicate additional public park space. |  Lisandro Orozco

[DisneylandForward Notice of Preparation / Initial Study](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/40211/Notice-of-Preparation--Initial-Study)   
[Project Site Map](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/40212/Project-Site-Map) [Scoping Meeting Presentation (November 4, 2021)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/40509/Scoping-Meeting-Presentation-November-4-2021) |  **PROJECT LOCATION** : The Project Site is located within an area known as The Anaheim Resort, a 1,078-acre portion of the City that is generally west of the U.S. Interstate 5 corridor, south of Vermont Avenue, east of Walnut Street, and north of Chapman Avenue within the City.  The Project site encompasses The Disneyland Resort and the following properties owned or controlled by Disney or other subsidiaries of The Walt Disney Company: 1515 S. Manchester Avenue (currently used as the Manchester Cast Member Lot); 1585 S. Manchester Avenue (currently an office building and the Manchester Cast Member Lot); 1530 S. Harbor Boulevard (currently used as the Manchester Cast Member Lot); 1900 S. Harbor Boulevard (currently used as the Toy Story Parking Lot); 333 W. Ball Road (currently used as the Harbor Cast Member Lot); and 1717 S. Disneyland Drive (Pixar Place Hotel); collectively “Disney property”. The Project Site also includes areas identified in the General Plan as planned extensions of Gene Autry Way between Harbor Boulevard and Haster Street and of Clementine Street between Katella Avenue and Orangewood Avenue, portions of which are outside The Anaheim Resort. **PROJECT DESCRIPTION:** The proposed project would allow the transfer of uses permitted under the previously approved Disneyland Resort Project to other areas of the Disneyland Resort Specific Plan (DRSP) and Disney’s properties in the Anaheim Resort Specific Plan (ARSP) (Project). The Project would not increase the amount of development square footage or hotel rooms currently allowed in the DRSP and analyzed in Environmental Impact Report (EIR) No. 311, which the City of Anaheim (City) certified in 1993, or the number of hotel rooms currently allowed in the ARSP and analyzed in Supplemental Environmental Impact Report No. 340, which the City certified in 2012. However, the Project would permit an increase of 4,376 theme park-related parking spaces in the ARSP. The Project also would provide for future administrative review by the City of Disney’s development projects on Disney Properties, which would rely on this Draft Subsequent Environmental Impact Report in accordance with CEQA standards. The Project proposes to modify the limits of the existing Theme Park and Hotel District boundaries within the existing DRSP perimeter, rename Districts within the DRSP, and establish Overlays for Disney’s ARSP Properties. Disney is not seeking additional square footage for theme park uses or retail entertainment uses or additional hotel rooms within The Disneyland Resort as part of this Project. Instead, the Project would allow Disney to move the existing approvals to other Disney property governed by the DRSP and ARSP. Approval of the Project would include amendments to the City of Anaheim General Plan, Bicycle Master Plan, DRSP, ARSP, Anaheim Resort Public Realm Landscape Program, Anaheim Resort Identity Program and Anaheim Commercial Recreation Area Maximum Permitted Structural Height Map. No changes are proposed to District A, or the Anaheim GardenWalk, or C-R Overlays, of the DRSP or Hotel Circle Specific Plan.   
|  Elaine Thienprasiddhi  
Phone: 714-765-4568  
DEV2020-00125 ocV!BE   
[NOD 9-27-22](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/46188/)   
[NOD 10-4-22](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/46290/)   
[Addendum No. 11 to SEIR No. 339](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/45716/)   
[Additional Information](https://www.anaheim.net/5157/) |  **PROJECT LOCATION** : The Platinum Triangle consists of approximately 820 acres at the confluence of Interstate 5 (I-5) and State Route 57 (SR-57) in the south-central portion of the City of Anaheim (“City”), in Orange County, California. The existing Platinum Triangle’s boundaries are the Southern Pacific Railroad and Cerritos Avenue to the north, the Santa Ana River to the east, the City’s boundary with the City of Orange to the south, and I‐5 to the west. The Platinum Triangle Overlay Zone consists of nine districts: Arena, ARTIC, Katella, Stadium, Gateway, Gene Autry, Orangewood, Lewis, and Office Districts.   
The approximately 100-acre ocV!be project site is generally bounded by State Route 57 (SR-57) to the west, (excluding the parcel at the southwest corner of Katella Avenue and Douglass Road), the Santa Ana River to the east, the confluence of the SR-57 and Santa Ana River to the south, and the Southern California Edison easement to the north. Off-site improvement areas include the State Route 57 (SR-57) northbound Katella Avenue off-ramp and intersection, SR-57 southbound Ball Road off-ramp and intersection, and dedication and improvements to River Road.   
**PROJECT DESCRIPTION:** The proposed project would construct ocV!be, a world-class mixed use lifestyle, entertainment, and residential neighborhood anchored by the existing Honda Center arena and ARTIC transit hub, consisting of the following: 1,922,776 square feet (s.f.) of commercial uses including entertainment and commercial indoor and outdoor recreation venues (including new indoor and outdoor live performance theaters, bars and nightclubs, bowling lanes, dancing venues, an amphitheater and outdoor seasonal events), retail, and restaurant uses including the existing Honda Center; 961,055 s.f. of office uses including the existing Arena Corporate Center; 250,000 s.f. of institutional uses including the existing Anaheim Regional Transportation Intermodal Center (ARTIC); 1,500 residential dwelling units including 195 affordable dwelling units proposed in connection with a Density Bonus request; parks and open space including a new public park (Meadow Park); parking areas; pedestrian bridges crossing Katella Avenue and Douglass Road; and, changes to the roadway network (including a new public street between Katella Avenue and Ball Road proposed to be named River Road, the abandonment of Douglass Road between Katella Avenue and Cerritos Avenue and new private streets). |  Scott Koehm   
Phone: 714-765-5395

  5. Community Emergency Response Team (CERT)


#  Community Emergency Response Team (CERT) 
Anaheim CERT promotes the ongoing training and participation of our volunteer force by providing many educational opportunities to better prepare for and respond to disasters. CERT certified volunteers with the City of Anaheim fall under 3 different levels of training to prepare volunteers to respond in a variety of areas during a disaster situation.  
  
Disasters can happen at any time in Anaheim. We experience earthquakes, fires and flooding. Are you prepared to handle an emergency that is large in scale?  

Training  
Individuals, neighborhoods, businesses and schools may be on their own for 72 hours or more. CERT training will provide you with the necessary and practical skills to help yourself, your family and your neighborhood until trained emergency personnel arrive. CERT training is a course approved by the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA), and provides critical skills in emergency preparedness and response. As a member of a CERT team, you can respond to disasters and participate in drills and exercises.  
  
Educating Our Residents   
The Community Emergency Response Team (CERT) Program educates people about disaster preparedness for hazards that may impact their area and trains them in basic disaster response skills, such as fire safety, light search and rescue, team organization, and disaster medical operations.

Anaheim Community Services Department offers a variety of adult sports leagues and programs throughout the City.
[Basketball Leagues](https://www.anaheim.net/943/Basketball-Leagues)
[Football Leagues](https://www.anaheim.net/1867/Football-Leagues)
[Softball Leagues by Major League Softball](https://www.mlsoftball.com/programs/66002/anaheim)
[](https://anaheim.net/6442/Volleyball-Leagues)
[Pickleball Leagues](https://anaheim.net/6663/Pickleball-Leagues)
All of the sports leagues and programs are offered to adults 18 years of age and older. EVERYONE IS WELCOME! Residents and Non-Residents. If you are an individual looking to join a team we’ll do our best to match you up with a team looking to add players. On occasion, we are able to form a team of individuals. Click on one of the links above to get additional information about the leagues or to register a team.

  6. Basketball Leagues


#  Basketball Leagues 
The Anaheim Community Services Department offers basketball leagues for players 18 years and older. The leagues operate 4 seasons annually, with all teams scheduled to play a minimum of 10 games. Every season is concluded with a single elimination playoff.  
  
For more information and to register please visit [www.teamsideline.com/anaheim](http://www.teamsideline.com/anaheim).
## Seasons
Winter: January - March
Spring: April - June
Summer: July - September
Fall: September - December
## Cost
Team Registration: $365
Deposit to Reserve Spot: $100 (remaining balance of $265 due day after second game). 
Weekly Official’s Fees: $35 (paid in cash at gym)
## League Nights
Sunday Men’s at [Downtown Anaheim Gym](https://www.anaheim.net/2208/Downtown-Anaheim-Youth-Center), [East Anaheim Gym](https://www.anaheim.net/966/East-Anaheim-Gymnasium), and/or [West Anaheim Gym](https://www.anaheim.net/2209/West-Anaheim-Youth-Center).
Tuesday Men's at [East Anaheim Gym](https://www.anaheim.net/966/East-Anaheim-Gymnasium), 
Wednesday Men's at [East Anaheim Gym](https://www.anaheim.net/966/East-Anaheim-Gymnasium).
Locations may change based upon gym availability and demand.

  7. Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program


#  Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program 
## Recycle Your Refrigerator/Freezer & Receive $50
Anaheim Public Utilities customers can receive $50 for recycling their old spare refrigerator or freezer.
## How the Program Works
  1. Recycle your refrigerator or freezer (you will need to arrange an independent pickup service).
  2. Complete the [program application](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/53447/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-Application-English-and-Spanish-0224) or [apply online](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-334) to confirm program participation eligibility.
  3. You will receive your incentive check within six (6) weeks of application receipt and approval.


## Requirements
  * Must be Anaheim Public Utilities residential electric customers.
  * Only one operating unit may be recycled for incentive every five (5) years.
  * Recycled unit must be at minimum 10 cubic feet and a maximum 32 cubic feet. Mini refrigerators, such as wine coolers or dorm coolers, and commercial-sized refrigerators do not qualify.


Customers seeking information on bulky item collection services can be found [here](https://www.anaheim.net/6276/Bulky-Item-Collection). 
_*Program is subject to available funding._
![Refrigerator Recycling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27141)

714-765-4250 or Email

#  Home Utility Check-Up 
### For any questions or to learn more about the program, please call 714-765-4274.
* * *
Want to know how you can lower your utility bill? 
By participating in our **FREE Home Utility Check-Up** , our contracted staff will conduct a personalized analysis of your home energy and water use, and help you determine what actions you can take to be more efficient in your utility use.
**Please note that this is a FREE program and participation is always initiated by you, the customer.**
Staff will never actively visit your home or call you to solicit your participation, nor will they ever try to sell you additional services. 
If you have questions about someone offering a service claiming to be working in collaboration with Anaheim Public Utilities, please call 714-765-3300 to verify.
Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.
![LED](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=14810)
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

#  Be Fire Safe 
Properties in or near a fire threat zone are at a higher risk for wildfire damage. Be energy smart and protect your home by taking advantage of rebates on fire-resistant measures, planting approved shade trees, and following any [Anaheim Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/5629) recommendations to improve safety in wildfire zones.
![Picture1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64346)
![Picture2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64347)
* * *
## FIRE SAFETY REBATES
Anaheim Public Utilities provides rebates aimed to assist residents in saving money on fire safety upgrades.
  1. [Fire-Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_0)


This type of attic insulation is a layer of fiber that reduces the risk of fires starting in the attic while at the same time decrease the energy demands of heating and cooling systems. The effectiveness of this insulation is evaluated by its Class Fire Rating and R-Value, which quantifies heat exchange resistance. 
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Class 1 (A) Fire Rating 
  * (Invoice must detail Fire Spread 0-25)
  * R-30 rating or greater


**Incentive Amount**
  * $0.35 per sq. ft.
  * Rebate cap of $750


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28826)  
**Helpful Resources**
  * [Insulation Guidelines](https://www.energystar.gov/sites/default/files/asset/document/Technical%20Bulletin%20Achieving%20Grade%201%20Insulation%20in%20Fire-Rated%20Roofs.pdf)
  * [ENERGY STAR® Certified Insulation](https://www.energystar.gov/saveathome/seal_insulate/certified_insulation)
  * [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program](https://calfire.govmotus.org/BMLSearch/Index)


  1. [Fire-Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_1)


These types of vents are designed to block embers and fire from entering the home through the attic during extreme situations such as wildfires or local vegetation fires.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Installation of Fire Resistant Attic Insulation is recommended
  * Product must be approved under the [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program for use in Wildland Urban Interface Areas](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/fire-engineering-and-investigations/building-materials-listing)


**Incentive Amount**
  * $350 rebate


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25676/Home-Incentives-Application?bidId=)
![Ventsd](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28827)
  1. [Uninterruptible power supply (ups)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_2)


An uninterruptible power supply (UPS), also known as battery backup, provides emergency power to critical devices during an unforeseen power outage. An UPS provides power stored in a battery to allow for safe, orderly shutdown of connected equipment (e.g. computers). Medical device UPS systems are designed to provide an uninterrupted power source to keep your medical device running when it is supposed to such as CPAP Machines.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * ENERGY STAR® Certified (for standard UPS only)  



**Incentive Amount**
  * $50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)
  * $200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving Medical Lifeline Rate (Maximum of 2)


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=27539)
  

  1. [Battery storage](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_3)


Battery Storage provides Anaheim Public Utilities customers with the economic and environmental benefits while accelerating the adoption of renewable energy in your home by storing energy when the sun is out and using it when it's not. Battery Storage also allows a certain level of back-up power during a planned or unforeseen power outage.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Must be UL Certified
  * Energy Storage system must have minimum of 5kWh capacity 
  * Eligible Energy Storage Unit installation must be standalone or part of a future existing solar PV system
  * Energy Storage Unit shall be capable of storing energy produced from electrical energy, such as, solar PV energy, wind energy or some other residential produced electricity
  * Customer agrees to be on a [Time of Use](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25947/Schedule-TOU-2-?bidId=) rate and [MyPower Savings Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5920) for at least 12 months


**Incentive Amount**
  * Up to $3,000 per household 


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24)
![Home Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=34164)
* * *
## ADDITIONAL FIRE SAFETY RESOURCES
Anaheim residents can take advantage of programs designed to enhance fire safety and resilience.
  1. [Wildfire Home Assessment](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_0)


The Wildland Urban Interface is the zone of transition between unoccupied land and human development. 
Communities surrounded by wildlands are at varying degrees of risk from wildfires. Schedule a one-on-one consultation with an Anaheim Wildfire Mitigation Specialist to identify potential wildfire threats within your property and ways you can harden your home.
Schedule your FREE Wildfire Home Assessment
  1. [Fire-Resistant Shade Trees](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_1)


TreePower offers a number of FREE fire-resistant shade trees that meet fire threat zone guidelines.
![Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28816)  

  

**ZONE 1 -****within 30 feet of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Trim trees regularly to keep branches a minimum of 10 feet from other trees
  * Remove branches that hang over your roof and keep dead branches 10 feet away from your chimney
  * Remove or prune flammable plants and shrubs near windows


**ZONE 2 -****within 30-100 ft of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Remove vegetation and items that could catch fire from around and under decks
  * Remove fallen leaves, needles, twigs, bark, cones, and small branches
  * Create horizontal spacing between shrubs and trees
  * Eliminate opportunities for a vertical "fire ladder" by:
    * Removing branches beneath large trees for a 6-foot minimum clearance
    * Allowing a minimum clearance of 3 times height of shrub underneath a tree


[Get your FREE trees](https://www.anaheim.net/757/)  

* * *
[![Fire Threat Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=29986)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/29987/FireThreatZones_JPG_Draft3)
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

#  Energy Rebates 
Make your home more energy efficient by receiving a rebate to help offset the costs of purchasing qualified ENERGY STAR® appliances and home products.   

[![Appliance & Fixtures](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28489)](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/)
[![Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35178)](http://www.anaheim.net/5730/Battery-Storage)
[![Building Products](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28490)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/)
[![Heating & Cooling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28491)](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/)
[![backup power](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45136)](https://www.anaheim.net/5243)
[![Pool Equipment](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28493)](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/)
[![Fire Safety](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28861)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/)
# Energy Programs  

Participate in one of our programs to help you live a cleaner and greener lifestyle. We can help you recycle your old, operational refrigerator, perform an in-home evaluation of your electric and water usage, and even help you install a free shade tree for your home.   

[![A/C Tune-up](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28494)](https://www.anaheim.net/3436/)

Latest News
  1. [![Lakeview lane vertical 2-26-26 copy](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=69907)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3188)
####  [Heads up in east Anaheim: Plan for 91 Freeway ramp and lane closures ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3188)
Heads up in east Anaheim: Plan for 91 Freeway ramp and lane closures at Lakeview Avenue starting 9 p.m. on Friday, Feb. 27, through 4 a.m. Monday, March 2. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3188)
  2. [![The Anaheim Resort](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3167)
####  [Anaheim approves hotelier plan to fund affordable housing for workers ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3167)
Hotels around Anaheim’s theme parks and convention center will fund affordable housing for hospitality workers with final action Tuesday at Anaheim’s City Council meeting. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3167)
  3. [![city news graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
####  [Latest: updated hearing date for Ament motion to withdraw guilty plea; running timeline ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
Anaheim has condemned actions documented in a federal investigation of the city's former mayor and a former Anaheim Chamber of Commerce executive that came to light in 2022. Here is what has happened, how Anaheim has responded and ongoing updates. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
  4. [![Image to go with a press release for Anaheim Contigo and information on immigration enforcement](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
####  [Anaheim Contigo: go to for timely immigration enforcement information ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
Anaheim Contigo is your source for timely information about federal immigration enforcement activity in our city. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
  5. [![20250514-DSC00828](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
####  [New state law covers space near crosswalks for safer streets ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
A new California law limits how close you can park near crosswalks in Anaheim neighborhoods and across the state. The state law aims to improve visibility and make streets safer for kids, families and everyone who walks, bikes or drives. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
  6. [![center greens ](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
####  [Center Greens Park debuts downtown ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
Center Greens Park debuted downtown Saturday, transforming a grassy, informal space into an iconic park with a new skate park, walking paths, butterfly garden and more for community to enjoy. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
  7. [![news flash La Palma groundbreaking](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
####  [La Palma Park Way Promenade Groundbreaking ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
Steeped in history, La Palma Park promenade project starts multiyear transformation of iconic park [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
  8. [![two volunteers building a rock climbing wall for new playground at little people's park ](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
####  [Iconic downtown Anaheim park gets new kid-designed, volunteer-built, donated playground ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
One of Anaheim’s most iconic parks got a new playground designed by neighborhood kids and built in a day by more than 100 volunteers from The CarMax Foundation, KABOOM!, Anaheim Family YMCA and the city of Anaheim. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
  9. [![city news graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)
####  [Anaheim shares state audit, supports recommendations with adoption underway ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)
Anaheim has shared a state audit critical of contract and funding oversight involving Visit Anaheim and the Anaheim Chamber of Commerce and welcomes the report’s recommendations with adoption already underway. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)

  7. Rebate Guidelines & Application


#  Rebate Guidelines & Application 
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a wide variety of rebates to assist our residential customers with energy savings. Please feel free to read through our application process and program guidelines below before completing the online [application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/). If you prefer to apply using a paper application, please contact us and we would be happy to send you one.  

  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)
  2. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)
  3. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)


After purchasing a qualifying conservation measure or ENERGY STAR® certified replacement appliance, please follow the steps below:
  1. Collect all appropriate supporting documents such as original, paid sales receipts or invoices, which must include the product’s brand name, the manufacturer model number, the purchase price and date, and, if available, the product’s efficiency information.
  2. Apply online by clicking the link and creating an account.
  3. Follow the instructions within the online rebate processing tool.
  4. Once you have submitted your application online, you can check its status by logging into your rebate account. Please note that applications will be denied if no return communication is received from the customer after three outreach attempts and/or within one year from the submission date.
  5. Paper applications may be requested by contacting by phone, email, or downloading online, [ENGLISH ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64054/Home-Incentives-Application_April_2025)or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64053/Home-Incentives-Application-SPANISH_April_2025)
  6. An Anaheim Public Utilities representative may call to schedule a physical inspection of your installed appliance or conservation measure before issuing a rebate.
  7. We ask that you try to keep your inspection appointment to ensure that your application is processed in a timely manner. If you need to reschedule, please contact Anaheim Public Utilities at 714-408-4161.
  8. Rebate checks are normally mailed within 30 days from the date of approval.


*If you are applying for ENERGY STAR certified window rebates, you must also include a [window incentive worksheet (PDF)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/3723) (completed by your contractor) and the stickers that display the actual ultraviolet (UV) value and solar heat gain coefficient (SHGC).
  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)


  * **Residential Incentive** : Home Incentive Programs provide customers with rebates to help lower the cost of investing in high efficiency products and measures. The incentive offer is limited to residential customer for residential use. The appliance must be installed in a residential dwelling within Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) service area. A residential dwelling is defined a single family home, condominium, apartment, or mobile home permanently located within APU service area. The dwelling must be fully constructed and occupied (no new construction).
  * **Required documentation** : This application and a paid sales receipt for each product must be returned to the Rebate Processing Center office listed on the bottom of the application. Online purchases require submittal of the delivery slip. To expedite your application, please upload a copy of your APU bill. Window rebates require submittal of a window incentive worksheet.
  * **Limitations** : In order to qualify for the Program, Applicant must be an APU electric service customer with any and all APU accounts in good standing. The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-service basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by APU. Maximum of one application per measure type per account service address every 5 years, unless approved by APU. The incentive amount will not exceed the purchase price. The incentive check will be mailed in approximately 30 days following application approval.
  * **Installation verification** : Applicant understands and agrees that APU or its representative may inspect the installed product for installation verification before the incentive is paid.
  * **Delinquent accounts** : Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the APU’s Rates, Rules and Regulations) utility bill(s) for which the Applicant is responsible whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant understands APU may withhold payment of incentive until all of the conditions listed herein are met.
  * APU does not warrant, endorse, or assume liability for the quality, performance, or safety of the contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the applicants responsibility.
  * Applicant is responsible for meeting all program requirements and for checking with state/county/city governments and homeowner’s association (if any) in the area regarding local conditions, restrictions, codes, ordinances, rules, and regulations prior to installation. 
  * Applicant must submit application within 1 year of purchase of the product.
  * Note that insufficient or incorrect data may delay your rebate check.
  * The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-serve basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by Anaheim Public Utilities.


  1. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


For assistance with your application, or more information on qualifying criteria or the status of your rebate application, please call our rebate processing team at 714-408-4161, which is open Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., excluding holidays.
### More Information
Visit the [Environmental Protection Agency's ENERGY STAR® website](http://www.energystar.gov/) for more information.
  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)

#  Private Use EV Charger Rebate 
## Receive up to $300 or $600
_Rebates are subject to fund availability._
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a rebate to customers who **install a Level 2 plug-in Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger at their home or business**. 
1. Up to $300 for a Level 2 EV charger 
2. Up to $600 for a networked charger and the customer must sign up for a time-of-use rate (TOU)
See the [TOU Rates](https://www.anaheim.net/6335/Residential-Rates) page for rates.  
  
Eligible expenses for reimbursement include:
    * Cost of the charger
    * Cost of installation (Material receipts are not eligible for reimbursement) 
    * Upgrades needed to accommodate the charger 
    * City permit fees for the EV charger


[![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)
  

  1. [Program Steps](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_0)


## Plug-In Electric Vehicle Charger Rebate Program Steps
  1. Obtain an Anaheim building permit for the installation of your new EV charger. For questions about the permitting process, call**Anaheim Building Services at 714-765-5153**


    * New residential developments may be 'EV Ready' or 'EV Capable,' meaning the space meets building code requirements for the future installation of electric vehicle supply equipment.
    * A resident can move into a new development that is 'EV Ready,' but this doesn't necessarily mean the parking space comes with the actual EV charging equipment. 
    * When the resident installs the level 2 EV charging equipment, they will need to attain a permit for the hardware. 
    * To qualify for the EV rebate for level 2 chargers, residents must attain a permit for the EV charging station. Anaheim Municipal Code Section 15.05.040 requires each EV charging station to be issued a permit.


2. Install your EV charger in accordance with building codes.
3. Complete the [online application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/).
For questions, call 714-765-4250 or email evrebates@anaheim.net. 
**Required documents include:**
  * All charging station purchase and installation receipts
  * Copy of Building Department Permit Card showing final sign-off
  * Copy of your Anaheim Public Utilities bill
  * Photo of the installed EV charging station
  * Photo of the serial number on your charger


  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_1)


  * Anaheim Public Utilities does not warrant, endorse or assume liability for the quality, performance or safety of the Customer selected contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the customer’s responsibility.
  * Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the City of Anaheim _Rates, Rules and Regulations_) utility bill(s) for which the customer is responsible, whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant is responsible for obtaining all required permits from the City of Anaheim.
  * Applicant must receive electric service from Anaheim Public Utilities.
  * Applicant must submit all required documents listed..
  * Applicant understands and agrees that Anaheim Public Utilities may inspect the installed EV charger before the incentive is paid.
  * Maximum of 1 EV charger rebate per customer.


Prior to installation, the applicant is responsible for meeting all Program requirements and for checking with state, county, or city governments and homeowner’s associations (if any) regarding: Codes,Conditions, Ordinances, Restrictions, Rules and regulations
  * The EV charger must be installed within Anaheim Public Utilities service area.

#  About the Utilities 
## Overview
  * Largest population in Orange County
  * 10th largest population in California
  * Area: 50 square miles
  * Average annual rainfall: 13.4 inches
  * Average annual temperature: 67 degrees
  * Annual visitors: more than 25 million
  * Population: 360,000


Anaheim residents are more than utilities customers, they are owners of their utilities. They provide input to the decision process both directly and through an appointed resident advisory Public Utilities Board. With final authority vested in Anaheim's elected City Council, decisions are made in the best interest of our residents, quality of life, and local economy. As a municipal, not-for-profit utilities, our rates are based on our costs of providing water and electricity. 
## Local Ownership, Local Control
  

Anaheim Public Utilities is a city-owned, not-for-profit electric and water utility that offers quality electric and water services to residents and businesses in Anaheim at rates among the lowest in Orange County.
[![Electric Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31367)](https://www.anaheim.net/2104/)
[![Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31366)](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)
[![Who governs the Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31219)](https://www.anaheim.net/1930/)
## Anaheim has been hard at work serving its residents ever since its foundation in 1857
  
For more than 150 years, the city has cultivated the ability to rise to any challenge with innovative solutions to better the community. In 1879, Anaheim’s leaders took responsibility for operating the water system for their fledgling city. Then, in 1894, they seized the opportunity to build the first municipal electric utility in Southern California, changing the face of the region for years to come. 
Such reliable, affordable water and power attracted businesses and families, evolving Anaheim into a leading industrial and manufacturing center. In the 1950s, the city’s many unique qualities attracted the attention of the famous Walt Disney, who was looking for the perfect place to build his dream park. Anaheim boldly embraced the opportunity, and was soon catapulted onto the world stage - first as a dynamic tourist destination and later as a bustling convention, sports and entertainment hub. 
As a result, the Anaheim of today looks much different from the Anaheim of the past — and its demands for water and electricity are far higher than our ancestors could ever have imagined. But the dedication of the city to serving its community and meeting challenges head-on has remained unchanged. Our residential electric rates are the lowest in Orange County, and we still work hard to deliver quality water at a competitive price. We also offer a value-packed array of [rebates and incentives](https://www.anaheim.net/404/Rebates-Incentives) — helping our customers make more efficient and economical use of the critical resources we provide.
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)
  1. [About Electric Services](https://www.anaheim.net/2104/)
  2. [About Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)
  3. [Facilities Tour Book](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/35601/Anaheim-Public-Utilities---Facilities-Tour-Book)

#  Departments 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [City Administration](https://www.anaheim.net/149/City-Administration)
The City of Anaheim is a City Council/City Manager form of government.
  2. ####  [City Attorney](https://www.anaheim.net/439/City-Attorney)
View the services the City Attorney provides the City of Anaheim.
  3. ####  [Boards & Commissions](https://www.anaheim.net/176/Boards-Commissions)
The City of Anaheim has a number of boards and commissions on which citizens serve and make recommendations to the City Council in an advisory capacity. 
  4. ####  [City Clerk](https://www.anaheim.net/218/City-Clerk)
The City Clerk is responsible for elections, records, and passport services.
  5. ####  [City Council](https://www.anaheim.net/173/City-Council)
The City of Anaheim aims to continually reinvent itself to be a better place for all who live, do business in, or visit our great city.
  6. ####  [Community Services](https://www.anaheim.net/6094/Community-Services)
The goal of the Community Services Department is to enrich individuals, families, and the community through the provision of services, facilities, and programs which improve the quality of life in Anaheim.
  7. ####  [Convention, Sports & Entertainment](https://www.anaheim.net/2595/Convention-Sports-Entertainment)
The Anaheim Convention Center, Sports and Entertainment consists of entertainment facilities such as Angel Stadium of Anaheim, the Honda Center, the Anaheim Convention Center, the National Grove of Anaheim. It also includes the transportation facility known as ARTIC.
  8. ####  [Economic Development](https://www.anaheim.net/6280/Economic-Development)
Find a list of services the Economic Development Department provided.
  9. ####  [Finance](https://www.anaheim.net/479/Finance)
The Finance Department assists other departments in the city in meeting their service objectives by allocating and monitoring the city's financial resources, processing payroll and other financial transactions and procuring goods and services.
  10. ####  [Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/6096/Fire-Rescue)
Access the various fire and emergency services performed by Anaheim Fire & Rescue.
  11. ####  [Housing and Community Development](https://www.anaheim.net/6363/Housing-and-Community-Development)
The Community Development Department implements a variety of programs to foster a better quality of life. These programs create new residential opportunities, revitalize neighborhoods and create job training opportunities.
  12. ####  [Human Resources](https://www.anaheim.net/401/Human-Resources)
Human Resources partners with city departments to hire, compensate, support and create a workforce dedicated to delivering high-quality services, which makes Anaheim a special place to live, work and play.
  13. ####  [Planning & Building](https://www.anaheim.net/490/Planning-Building)
Find information about permits, building, licenses, code enforcement, economic development, historic preservation, and planning and zoning.
  14. ####  [Police Department](https://pd.anaheim.net/)
Access information about the Police Department's programs, what initiatives it's focusing on and more information about its different divisions.
  15. ####  [Public Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/6099/Public-Utilities)
Find more information about the Public Utilities Department, how to receive rebates and incentives and general information about city utilities.
  16. ####  [Public Works](https://www.anaheim.net/229/Public-Works)
Public Works provides a myriad of comprehensive services to meet a variety of the city's infrastructure, construction, and development needs.

#  Ways to Save 
Anaheim Public Utilities currently offers more than 45 rebates and incentive programs to assist Anaheim businesses, residents, income-qualified customers and schools save water, energy and money. Whether you are making capital improvements, replacing inefficient equipment or looking for free services on ways to help you save, we have a program that’s right for you!  

[![Residential](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44141)](https://www.anaheim.net/2532/)
### Single Family & Multi-Family Residents
[![Commercial](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44140)](https://www.anaheim.net/2533/)

  7. ENERGY STAR®


#  ENERGY STAR® 
ENERGY STAR®-qualified products and practices help you save electricity and money, as well as reduce greenhouse gas emissions by meeting strict energy efficiency guidelines set by the US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the US Department of Energy (DOE). The ENERGY STAR label designates superior energy performance in homes and buildings.   
  
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a range of incentives and rebates to encourage Anaheim residents to replace old, inefficient appliances and equipment with energy-efficient, ENERGY STAR-qualified appliances, to save energy and money.   
  
Our Home Incentives Program is specifically designed to encourage installation of ENERGY STAR-rated appliances - including refrigerators, air conditioning and lighting - and high-efficiency household conservation measures.  
  
More About ENERGY STAR  
You can learn more about ENERGY STAR at [energystar.gov](http://www.energystar.gov/).

#  Preparing for Wildfire 
![Preparing for wildfire header](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=33879)
## Are you ready for the next wildfire? We know it can be overwhelming to think about, which is why we’re making it easy for you to find the tools  
and resources you need to get started.
  

Click the following link to do a [Wildfire Defensible Space Self-Inspection](https://survey123.arcgis.com/share/f59c0935eeea4cd5a505bedf45128faa?portalUrl=https://gis.anaheim.net/portal).
  * [ Wildfire Defensible Space Self-Inspection ](https://survey123.arcgis.com/share/f59c0935eeea4cd5a505bedf45128faa?portalUrl=https://gis.anaheim.net/portal)


  * Wildfire Home Assessment


![Planning bar](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=33561)
  * [ Emergency Plan ](https://www.readyforwildfire.org/prepare-for-wildfire/get-set/wildfire-action-plan/)
  * [ Children ](https://www.readyforwildfire.org/prepare-for-wildfire/get-set/prepare-your-family/)


  * [ If You Left Right Now ](https://www.readyforwildfire.org/prepare-for-wildfire/go-evacuation-guide/)
  * [ Elderly, Disabled ](https://www.readyforwildfire.org/prepare-for-wildfire/get-set/prepare-your-family/)


  * [ Know Your Way ](http://www.anaheim.net/5204/Know-Your-Way)
  * [ Horse & Livestock Registration ](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Police-Department-13/Equine-and-Livestock-Evacuation-Registra-189)


![Prepare bar](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=33562)

####  [Sustainability Education Center Open House ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3177)
  2. [![wire theft news flash](https://www.anaheim.net/6099/Public-Utilities)](https://www.anaheim.net/6463/Wire-Theft-and-Vandalism)
####  [Wire Theft and Vandalism ](https://www.anaheim.net/6463/Wire-Theft-and-Vandalism)
Wire theft and vandalism are on the rise. This is a serious issue impacting the community and Anaheim Public Utilities needs your help to identify theft and report illegal activity. 
  3. [![News Flash-Fire Programs](https://www.anaheim.net/6099/Public-Utilities)](https://www.anaheim.net/5629/Preparing-for-Wildfire)
####  [Fire Safety Programs ](https://www.anaheim.net/5629/Preparing-for-Wildfire)
Living near California’s natural areas means facing a higher chance of wildfires. Knowing how to prepare is key to keeping your home and family safe.

#  Electric Vehicles 
### Anaheim Public Utilities offers EV rebates and programs to support our customer's path towards electrification. Whether you are new to the process or need hands-on support with your site plans, we're here to help you every step of the way. 
###   

### For more information about Anaheim Public Utilities' future adoption of EV’s in our community, please review our [Integrated Resources Plan](https://www.anaheim.net/4864) and [Southern California Association of Governments study](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50587/APU-Southern-California-Association-of-Governments-study).
###   

### Contact us by email or call 714-765-4250.
![drive green anaheim](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=46177)

#  Map & Directions 
Anaheim Public Utilities customer service, administrative, and water and electric engineering offices are located in Anaheim West Tower.
![A map to City Offices](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=2776)
## Anaheim West Tower
[201 South Anaheim Boulevard](https://www.google.com/maps/place/201+S+Anaheim+Blvd,+Anaheim,+CA+92805/@33.8346578,-117.9141443,17z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m6!3m5!1s0x80dcd624c5bf72d5:0xaf5ab8e0c3ce65a3!8m2!3d33.8346578!4d-117.9141443!16s%2Fg%2F11bw3y7gc0?sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwjrhO-os9iBAxW5JEQIHdlFB-IQ8gF6BAgQEAA&ved=2ahUKEwjrhO-os9iBAxW5JEQIHdlFB-IQ8gF6BAgZEAI&entry=tts)
## City Hall
[200 South Anaheim Boulevard](https://www.google.com/maps/place/200+S+Anaheim+Blvd,+Anaheim,+CA+92805/@33.8348023,-117.915163,17z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m6!3m5!1s0x80dcd63b2ffb8e2f:0x8b76fd3569286d51!8m2!3d33.8347979!4d-117.9125881!16s%2Fg%2F11bw3wzg0s?entry=tts)
### Contact Us
  1. 714-765-3300 or Email
## Call Center:
Mon - Thurs: 7 am - 6 pm  
Fri: 7 am - 5:30 pm
## In Person:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.   
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://www.anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)

#  About the Utilities 
## Overview
  * Largest population in Orange County
  * 10th largest population in California
  * Area: 50 square miles
  * Average annual rainfall: 13.4 inches
  * Average annual temperature: 67 degrees
  * Annual visitors: more than 25 million
  * Population: 360,000


Anaheim residents are more than utilities customers, they are owners of their utilities. They provide input to the decision process both directly and through an appointed resident advisory Public Utilities Board. With final authority vested in Anaheim's elected City Council, decisions are made in the best interest of our residents, quality of life, and local economy. As a municipal, not-for-profit utilities, our rates are based on our costs of providing water and electricity. 
## Local Ownership, Local Control
  

Anaheim Public Utilities is a city-owned, not-for-profit electric and water utility that offers quality electric and water services to residents and businesses in Anaheim at rates among the lowest in Orange County.
[![Electric Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31367)](https://www.anaheim.net/2104/)
[![Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31366)](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)
[![Who governs the Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31219)](https://www.anaheim.net/1930/)
## Anaheim has been hard at work serving its residents ever since its foundation in 1857
  
For more than 150 years, the city has cultivated the ability to rise to any challenge with innovative solutions to better the community. In 1879, Anaheim’s leaders took responsibility for operating the water system for their fledgling city. Then, in 1894, they seized the opportunity to build the first municipal electric utility in Southern California, changing the face of the region for years to come. 
Such reliable, affordable water and power attracted businesses and families, evolving Anaheim into a leading industrial and manufacturing center. In the 1950s, the city’s many unique qualities attracted the attention of the famous Walt Disney, who was looking for the perfect place to build his dream park. Anaheim boldly embraced the opportunity, and was soon catapulted onto the world stage - first as a dynamic tourist destination and later as a bustling convention, sports and entertainment hub. 
As a result, the Anaheim of today looks much different from the Anaheim of the past — and its demands for water and electricity are far higher than our ancestors could ever have imagined. But the dedication of the city to serving its community and meeting challenges head-on has remained unchanged. Our residential electric rates are the lowest in Orange County, and we still work hard to deliver quality water at a competitive price. We also offer a value-packed array of [rebates and incentives](https://www.anaheim.net/404/Rebates-Incentives) — helping our customers make more efficient and economical use of the critical resources we provide.
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

#  Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program 
## Recycle Your Refrigerator/Freezer & Receive $50
Anaheim Public Utilities customers can receive $50 for recycling their old spare refrigerator or freezer.
## How the Program Works
  1. Recycle your refrigerator or freezer (you will need to arrange an independent pickup service).
  2. Complete the [program application](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/53447/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-Application-English-and-Spanish-0224) or [apply online](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-334) to confirm program participation eligibility.
  3. You will receive your incentive check within six (6) weeks of application receipt and approval.


## Requirements
  * Must be Anaheim Public Utilities residential electric customers.
  * Only one operating unit may be recycled for incentive every five (5) years.
  * Recycled unit must be at minimum 10 cubic feet and a maximum 32 cubic feet. Mini refrigerators, such as wine coolers or dorm coolers, and commercial-sized refrigerators do not qualify.


Customers seeking information on bulky item collection services can be found [here](https://www.anaheim.net/6276/Bulky-Item-Collection). 
_*Program is subject to available funding._
![Refrigerator Recycling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27141)

714-765-4250 or Email  
  
TTY/TDD: Dial 711 and ask the TRS operator to connect you to 714-765-3300.

Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm

#  Home Utility Check-Up 
### For any questions or to learn more about the program, please call 714-765-4274.
* * *
Want to know how you can lower your utility bill? 
By participating in our **FREE Home Utility Check-Up** , our contracted staff will conduct a personalized analysis of your home energy and water use, and help you determine what actions you can take to be more efficient in your utility use.
**Please note that this is a FREE program and participation is always initiated by you, the customer.**
Staff will never actively visit your home or call you to solicit your participation, nor will they ever try to sell you additional services. 
If you have questions about someone offering a service claiming to be working in collaboration with Anaheim Public Utilities, please call 714-765-3300 to verify.
Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.

[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

#  Energy Rebates 
Make your home more energy efficient by receiving a rebate to help offset the costs of purchasing qualified ENERGY STAR® appliances and home products.   

[![Appliance & Fixtures](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28489)](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/)
[![Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35178)](http://www.anaheim.net/5730/Battery-Storage)
[![Building Products](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28490)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/)
[![Heating & Cooling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28491)](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/)
[![backup power](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45136)](https://www.anaheim.net/5243)
[![Pool Equipment](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28493)](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/)
[![Fire Safety](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28861)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/)
# Energy Programs  

Participate in one of our programs to help you live a cleaner and greener lifestyle. We can help you recycle your old, operational refrigerator, perform an in-home evaluation of your electric and water usage, and even help you install a free shade tree for your home.   

[![A/C Tune-up](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28494)](https://www.anaheim.net/3436/)

  7. Fire Safety 


#  Be Fire Safe 
Properties in or near a fire threat zone are at a higher risk for wildfire damage. Be energy smart and protect your home by taking advantage of rebates on fire-resistant measures, planting approved shade trees, and following any [Anaheim Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/5629) recommendations to improve safety in wildfire zones.
![Picture1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64346)
![Picture2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64347)
* * *
## FIRE SAFETY REBATES
Anaheim Public Utilities provides rebates aimed to assist residents in saving money on fire safety upgrades.
  1. [Fire-Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_0)


This type of attic insulation is a layer of fiber that reduces the risk of fires starting in the attic while at the same time decrease the energy demands of heating and cooling systems. The effectiveness of this insulation is evaluated by its Class Fire Rating and R-Value, which quantifies heat exchange resistance. 
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Class 1 (A) Fire Rating 
  * (Invoice must detail Fire Spread 0-25)
  * R-30 rating or greater


**Incentive Amount**
  * $0.35 per sq. ft.
  * Rebate cap of $750


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28826)  
**Helpful Resources**
  * [Insulation Guidelines](https://www.energystar.gov/sites/default/files/asset/document/Technical%20Bulletin%20Achieving%20Grade%201%20Insulation%20in%20Fire-Rated%20Roofs.pdf)
  * [ENERGY STAR® Certified Insulation](https://www.energystar.gov/saveathome/seal_insulate/certified_insulation)
  * [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program](https://calfire.govmotus.org/BMLSearch/Index)


  1. [Fire-Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_1)


These types of vents are designed to block embers and fire from entering the home through the attic during extreme situations such as wildfires or local vegetation fires.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Installation of Fire Resistant Attic Insulation is recommended
  * Product must be approved under the [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program for use in Wildland Urban Interface Areas](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/fire-engineering-and-investigations/building-materials-listing)


**Incentive Amount**
  * $350 rebate


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25676/Home-Incentives-Application?bidId=)
![Ventsd](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28827)
  1. [Uninterruptible power supply (ups)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_2)


An uninterruptible power supply (UPS), also known as battery backup, provides emergency power to critical devices during an unforeseen power outage. An UPS provides power stored in a battery to allow for safe, orderly shutdown of connected equipment (e.g. computers). Medical device UPS systems are designed to provide an uninterrupted power source to keep your medical device running when it is supposed to such as CPAP Machines.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * ENERGY STAR® Certified (for standard UPS only)  



**Incentive Amount**
  * $50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)
  * $200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving Medical Lifeline Rate (Maximum of 2)


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=27539)
  

  1. [Battery storage](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_3)


Battery Storage provides Anaheim Public Utilities customers with the economic and environmental benefits while accelerating the adoption of renewable energy in your home by storing energy when the sun is out and using it when it's not. Battery Storage also allows a certain level of back-up power during a planned or unforeseen power outage.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Must be UL Certified
  * Energy Storage system must have minimum of 5kWh capacity 
  * Eligible Energy Storage Unit installation must be standalone or part of a future existing solar PV system
  * Energy Storage Unit shall be capable of storing energy produced from electrical energy, such as, solar PV energy, wind energy or some other residential produced electricity
  * Customer agrees to be on a [Time of Use](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25947/Schedule-TOU-2-?bidId=) rate and [MyPower Savings Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5920) for at least 12 months


**Incentive Amount**
  * Up to $3,000 per household 


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24)
![Home Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=34164)
* * *
## ADDITIONAL FIRE SAFETY RESOURCES
Anaheim residents can take advantage of programs designed to enhance fire safety and resilience.
  1. [Wildfire Home Assessment](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_0)


The Wildland Urban Interface is the zone of transition between unoccupied land and human development. 
Communities surrounded by wildlands are at varying degrees of risk from wildfires. Schedule a one-on-one consultation with an Anaheim Wildfire Mitigation Specialist to identify potential wildfire threats within your property and ways you can harden your home.
Schedule your FREE Wildfire Home Assessment
  1. [Fire-Resistant Shade Trees](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_1)


TreePower offers a number of FREE fire-resistant shade trees that meet fire threat zone guidelines.
![Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28816)  

  

**ZONE 1 -****within 30 feet of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Trim trees regularly to keep branches a minimum of 10 feet from other trees
  * Remove branches that hang over your roof and keep dead branches 10 feet away from your chimney
  * Remove or prune flammable plants and shrubs near windows


**ZONE 2 -****within 30-100 ft of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Remove vegetation and items that could catch fire from around and under decks
  * Remove fallen leaves, needles, twigs, bark, cones, and small branches
  * Create horizontal spacing between shrubs and trees
  * Eliminate opportunities for a vertical "fire ladder" by:
    * Removing branches beneath large trees for a 6-foot minimum clearance
    * Allowing a minimum clearance of 3 times height of shrub underneath a tree


[Get your FREE trees](https://www.anaheim.net/757/)  

* * *

#  Ways to Save 
Anaheim Public Utilities currently offers more than 45 rebates and incentive programs to assist Anaheim businesses, residents, income-qualified customers and schools save water, energy and money. Whether you are making capital improvements, replacing inefficient equipment or looking for free services on ways to help you save, we have a program that’s right for you!  

[![Residential](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44141)](https://www.anaheim.net/2532/)
